translations.description: From the final phase of a pull-up, we push ourselves back with our shoulders until we reach the front lever position. From there, we pull ourselves back up to the starting position.

translations.name: Ice Scream maker
translations.description: Dalla fase finale di una trazione alla sbarra e ci spingiamo indietro con le spalle fino ad arrivare in posizione di front lever. Da li trazioniamo di nuovo per riportarci nella posizione di partenza.

translations.name: Beinstrecker
translations.description:
• Muskulatur anspannen
  • Immer angewinkelt lassen
  • Nicht überstrecken
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Bewegung

translations.name: Extensión de cuádriceps
translations.description: Es un ejercicio de aislamiento centrado en los cuádriceps, que están formados por cuatro músculos específicos de la parte anterior del muslo: el recto femoral, el vasto lateral, el vasto medio y el vasto intermedio.

translations.name: Leg Extension
translations.description: Is an isolation exercise focused on the quadriceps, which are made up of four specific muscles on the front of your thigh—the rectus femoris, vastus lateralis, vastus medialis, and vastus intermedius.

translations.name: Front lever pull-up
translations.description: in the front lever position, with legs extended or easier if collected, pull by bringing the chest closer to the bar.

translations.name: Front lever tuck
translations.description: The muscles involved in the Front Lever, most subjected to stress, are mainly the extensors such as: the latissimus dorsi, the teres major, the posterior deltoid and the long head of the biceps.

translations.name: Front lever tuck
translations.description: I muscoli coinvolti nel Front Lever, maggiormente sottoposti a sforzo, sono principalmente gli estensori quali: il gran dorsale, il grande rotondo, il deltoide posteriore e il capolungo del bicipite

translations.name: Oblicuos en TRX
translations.description:
1.Place your feet in the stirrups and assume a high plank with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.

2.Pull your knees to your right elbow, then push them back out and to the centre.

translations.name: TRX Obliques
translations.description:
1. Place your feet in the stirrups and assume a high plank with your hands directly beneath your shoulders. 
  2. Pull your knees to your right elbow, then push them back out and to the centre.

translations.name: TRX hammer curl
translations.description: Place the grips as if they were a continuation of the straps. We will position our arms stretched forward and our body leaning back, completely straight. The biceps muscles will activate as you bend your elbows to bring your body towards the straps. The contraction movement will end with a slight outward movement of the hands, due to the grip we are using.

translations.name: TRX hammer curl
translations.description: Colocar los agarres como si de una continuación de las cintas se tratase. Colocaremos los brazos estirados hacia adelante y el cuerpo hacia atrás totalmente recto. Los músculos de los bíceps se activarán al doblar los codos para atraer el cuerpo hacia las cintas. El movimiento de contracción terminará con un ligero movimiento d

translations.name: Cable External Rotation
translations.description:
Steps:

  1. Start off placing an extension band around a post or in a secure position where it will not release and is at elbow level.
  2. Position yourself to the side of the band and with your hand that is opposite of the band, reach out and grab the handle.
  3. Bring the band to your chest keeping your elbow bent in a 90 degree angle then slowly rotate your arm in a backhand motion so that the band externally rotates out
  4. Continue out as far as possible so that you feel a stretch in your shoulders, hold for a count and then return back to the starting position.
  5. Repeat for as many reps and sets as desired.

translations.name: TRX gorilla biceps curl
translations.description: We'll stand facing the TRX straps and grab them with our fists facing each other, facing forward, with our arms fully extended at about shoulder-width apart. We'll keep our body straight, backward, as in the previous exercises, since we'll be lifting our body toward the TRX straps by activating our biceps. Our body will be straight, with our feet flat on the floor. This time, we'll place our arms open to the sides and at chest height. The movement we'll perform is a contraction toward our chest. To achieve this, we'll bend our elbows so that, by activating our biceps, we can pull our body toward the straps.

translations.name: TRX- biceps gorila
translations.description:
Nos colocaremos de frente a las cintas del TRX y las agarraremos con los puños de las manos enfrentados y al frente, con los brazos totalmente estirados a la altura más o menos de los hombros. El cuerpo lo mantendremos recto hacia atrás, como en los anteriores ejercicios, ya que lo que vamos a hacer es mediante la activación de los bíceps, elevar el cuerpo hacia las cintas del TRX.
El cuerpo estará recto y los pies apoyados en el suelo. Los brazos en esta ocasión, los vamos a colocar abiertos hacia los lados, y a la altura del pecho. El movimiento que vamos a hacer será una contracción hacia el pecho. Para conseguirlo, lo que haremos será doblar los codos para que, mediante la activación de los bíceps atraigamos el cuerpo hacia las cintas.

translations.name: TRX - curl de bíceps a un brazo
translations.description:
colocar las cintas cruzadas, para dejar toda la fuerza en una sola cuerda y que sea más seguro el ejercicio.

Para su realización nos vamos a colocar inclinados lateralmente. El brazo con el que vamos a trabajar será el que agarre la cinta del TRX que hemos montado combinando los dos agarres. El otro brazo lo dejaremos relajado lateralmente, ya que no actuará en este momento, pues sólo entrará en acción la parte del bíceps del brazo que tenemos agarrado a la cinta. El cuerpo, al igual que anteriormente, permanecerá recto, mirando al frente y cuanto más nos inclinemos mayor será la resistencia que tengamos que vencer durante el ejercicio. Ambas piernas, en este caso, estarán juntas para solamente mantener el brazo estirado para doblarlo por el codo. Este movimiento activará el bíceps y con ello trabajaremos esta parte del brazo.

translations.name: Trx Single Arm Bicep Curl
translations.description: Step 1: Stand comfortably and grasp the handles of the suspension system. Step 2: Lean back resting your weight on one arm, keepng the spine neutral. Step 3: Slowly curl to pull your body weight to the up position. Supinate the arm and squeeze as you approach the end position. Step 4: Lower yourself back down and repeat.

translations.name: Back bridge
translations.description: Lie on the floor with your back resting on the floor and your legs bent, feet flat on the floor, leaving about 20 cm between your feet and your buttocks. Your hands remain flat on the floor, pressed against your ears, with your fingers pointing toward your toes. Your elbows are bent and pointing toward the ceiling. This is the starting position. Now push your hips as high as you can, raising your body off the floor, and continue pushing through your hands, extending your elbows and knees, until your back is fully arched (lumbar bridge). Your head also comes off the floor and should be level with your elbows, looking back. This is the final position. The descent should be slow and controlled. Your spine should be in a convex curve. It's not good to lift your back off the floor; it should remain straight. It should be well rounded. You'll also notice a feeling of increased oxygenation in your back. After lifting off the floor, your hips should be very high, even above the level of your head. Your arms and legs should remain straight. It seems easy, but it requires a good level of flexibility. Breathing should be deep. This posture stretches the rib cage and puts pressure on the diaphragm. If the posture is performed correctly, breathing will be perfect. Never hold your breath during the exercise.

translations.name: Puente con propio peso
translations.description: Túmbate en el suelo apoyando la espalda en el suelo y las piernas flexionadas con los pies apoyados en el suelo, dejando unos 20 cm de distancia entre los pies y el culo. Las manos permanecen apoyadas en el suelo, pegadas a las orejas, con los dedos apuntando los pies. Los codos flexionados, y apuntando al techo. Esta es la posición inicial. Ahora empuja las caderas tan alto como puedas, elevando el cuerpo del suelo, y prosigue empujando a través de las manos, extendiendo los codos y las rodillas, hasta que la espalda esté bien arqueada (puente lumbar). La cabeza también despega del suelo, y debe quedar a la altura de los codos, mirando hacia atrás. Esta es la posición final. El descenso debe ser lento y controlado. La columna debe estar en curvatura convexa. No valen despegar la espalda del suelo, y que ésta quede recta. Debe de estar bien redondeada. Se nota además una sensación de oxigenación mayor en la espalda. Las caderas, tras despegarse del suelo, deben de estar bien altas, incluso por encima del nivel de la cabeza. Los brazos y las piernas deben permanecer rectos. Parece fácil, pero requiere de un buen nivel de flexibilidad. La respiración debe ser profunda. Esta postura estira la caja torácica, y pone el diafragma bajo presión. Si la postura está bien realizada, la respiración será perfecta. Nunca hay que mantener la respiración durante el ejercicio.

translations.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
translations.description: Sit on bench. Grasp dumbbell between feet. Place back of upper arm to inner thigh. Lean into leg to raise elbow slightly.

translations.name: Konzentrations-Curls
translations.description: Setz dich auf die Bank. Fassen Sie die Hantel zwischen den Beinen. Legen Sie den Oberarm an den inneren Oberschenkel. Unterarm anheben.

translations.name: TRX Tricep Extension
translations.description:
Attach the TRX or other suspension system overhead, having the handles hanging at chest level or slightly lower. Experiment to see what height allows you to set up in the best way.
Stand in front of the handles and grab them with your palms facing down.
Engage your abs, squeeze your glutes, take a breath and lean forward, lifting your heels off the floor. Keep your elbows straight.
Take another breath and lower yourself slowly by bending your elbows. 
Go down until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle (to the point where your wrists are above your elbows). Hold the position for a moment.
Extend your arms by engaging your triceps and bring yourself to the starting position as you exhale.

translations.name: extensiones de triceps con trx
translations.description:
POSICIÓN INICIAL:

Suspenda su TRX a la altura de la rodilla. Agarre las asas con un agarre prono y coloque sus piernas juntas detrás de usted. Su posición debe verse como una posición de push up. Mantenga su cuerpo en línea recta y sus brazos completamente extendidos retrayendo los omóplatos. Contraiga el suelo pelvico y el core mientras mantiene su cuerpo recto. Mueva sus manos para colocarlas juntas. Las cuerdas del TRX deben estar justo por encima de su cabeza y que deberían tener tensión.

EJECUCIÓN:

Comience a bajar doblando los codos hasta que las manos toquen la frente mientras inhala. No separe los codos entre ellos. Asegúrese de sólo mover los codos cuando realice el ejercicio. Empuje su torso hacia la posición inicial apretando el resto del cuerpo mientras exhala. Asegúrese de que sus escápulas siempre están retraídas.

translations.name: Fondos en TRX
translations.description: Los fondos se realizan con las manos en cada uno de los agarres de nuestro arnés y despegamos los pies desde el suelo para quedarnos completamente en suspensión. Desde ahí podremos ir haciendo fondos en suspensión exactamente igual que cuando se hacen en anillas o paralelas. Sin embargo, uno de los beneficios que tienen los fondos en TRX es que aumentan la inestabilidad y nos hacen realizar un esfuerzo más intenso.

translations.name: TRX dips
translations.description:
Start with the hands on the suspension trainer and feet on the ground.
Your hands should be under your shoulders with the feet slightly in front.
Lower yourself by bringing the hips between the handles and flexing the elbows.
Extend the elbows to come back to the starting position and repeat.

translations.name: Low Pulley Cable fFly
translations.description:
Adjust the cable machine: Set the pulley to the lowest setting possible.
Choose your handles: You can use neutral grip handles (palms facing each other) or straight handles depending on your preference. Neutral grip might be easier on your wrists.
Stand with proper form: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged. Maintain a slight arch in your lower back throughout the exercise.
Grip the handles: Grab the handles with your chosen grip and step back a small step or two until there's slight tension on the cables.
Initiate the movement: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the exercise. Imagine you're giving someone a big hug. Squeeze your chest muscles as you bring your hands together in front of your chest.
Control the movement: Focus on squeezing your chest muscles rather than using your arms to pull the handles.
Peak contraction: Briefly hold the squeeze at the top of the movement with your hands together at chest level.
Return slowly: Slowly release the tension and return the handles back down to the starting position with your arms slightly extended but not locked out.

translations.name: Low Pulley Cable fFly
translations.description:
Adjust the cable machine: Set the pulley to the lowest setting possible.
Choose your handles: You can use neutral grip handles (palms facing each other) or straight handles depending on your preference. Neutral grip might be easier on your wrists.
Stand with proper form: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged. Maintain a slight arch in your lower back throughout the exercise.
Grip the handles: Grab the handles with your chosen grip and step back a small step or two until there's slight tension on the cables.
Initiate the movement: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the exercise. Imagine you're giving someone a big hug. Squeeze your chest muscles as you bring your hands together in front of your chest.
Control the movement: Focus on squeezing your chest muscles rather than using your arms to pull the handles.
Peak contraction: Briefly hold the squeeze at the top of the movement with your hands together at chest level.
Return slowly: Slowly release the tension and return the handles back down to the starting position with your arms slightly extended but not locked out.

translations.name: Croci ai Cavi Bassi
translations.description:
1. Regolare la macchina a cavo: Impostare la puleggia sulla posizione più bassa possibile.
  2. Scegliere le maniglie: Potete usare maniglie a presa neutra (con i palmi rivolti l'uno verso l'altro) o diritta, a seconda delle vostre preferenze. La presa neutra potrebbe essere più facile per i polsi.
  3. Mettetevi in piedi con una posizione corretta: State in piedi con i piedi alla larghezza delle spalle, le ginocchia leggermente piegate e il core impegnato. Mantenete un leggero arco nella parte bassa della schiena per tutta la durata dell'esercizio.
  4. Afferrare le maniglie: Afferrare le maniglie con la presa prescelta e indietreggiare di uno o due passi fino a quando i cavi sono in leggera tensione.
  5. Iniziare il movimento: Mantenete i gomiti leggermente piegati per tutta la durata dell'esercizio. Immaginate di abbracciare qualcuno. Stringete i muscoli del petto mentre avvicinate le mani davanti al petto.
  6. Controllare il movimento: Concentratevi sulla compressione dei muscoli del petto piuttosto che sull'uso delle braccia per tirare le maniglie.
  7. Picco di contrazione: Mantenere brevemente la contrazione al culmine del movimento con le mani unite all'altezza del petto.
  8. Ritornare lentamente: Rilasciare lentamente la tensione e riportare le maniglie alla posizione di partenza con le braccia leggermente distese ma non bloccate.

translations.name: Low Pulley Cable fFly
translations.description:
1. Adjust the cable machine: Set the pulley to the lowest setting possible.
  2. Choose your handles: You can use neutral grip handles (palms facing each other) or straight handles depending on your preference. Neutral grip might be easier on your wrists.
  3. Stand with proper form: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged. Maintain a slight arch in your lower back throughout the exercise.
  4. Grip the handles: Grab the handles with your chosen grip and step back a small step or two until there's slight tension on the cables.
  5. Initiate the movement: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the exercise. Imagine you're giving someone a big hug. Squeeze your chest muscles as you bring your hands together in front of your chest.
  6. Control the movement: Focus on squeezing your chest muscles rather than using your arms to pull the handles.
  7. Peak contraction: Briefly hold the squeeze at the top of the movement with your hands together at chest level.
  8. Return slowly: Slowly release the tension and return the handles back down to the starting position with your arms slightly extended but not locked out.

translations.name: Pullover
translations.description: Doubling as a back and chest exercise, the Dumbbell Pullover can train both your pecs and lats.

translations.name: Sitting Calf Stretch (Dorsiflexion)
translations.description: This is a light stretch for the calf that is great for rehab.

translations.name: Plantarflexion Stretch with Band
translations.description: This stretch is for the ankles, as well as Tibialis anterior.

translations.name: Incline Dumbbell Bench Press
translations.description: The incline dumbbell press is a mixture of the dumbbell chest press and the shoulder press, and both the front deltoids and the upper portions of the chest muscles are worked in this exercise

translations.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
translations.description: The incline dumbbell press is a mixture of the dumbbell chest press and the shoulder press, and both the front deltoids and the upper portions of the chest muscles are worked in this exercise.

translations.name: Press inclinado con mancuernas
translations.description: Tumbado en el banco inclinado, con los pies apoyados en el suelo, una mancuerna en cada mano, agarre en pronación. Sostén las mancuernas a cada lado a la altura del pecho. Tus antebrazos deben estar perpendiculares al suelo.

translations.name: Dumbbell Chest Press
translations.description: The dumbbell chest press is a pressing exercise similar to the barbell bench press, except that it is performed with dumbbells.

translations.name: Biceps Close Grip Pull Down
translations.description: On a lat pull down machine, hold the bar keeping your hands relatively close. Use an underhand grip (I.E.: the back of your hand must be facing the machine). Then pull down the bar in a straight line towards the ground. Make sure your biceps are the main drivers of the motion. You will probably feel your shoulders and lats working, but make sure your biceps are working more.

translations.name: Puxada de Biceps
translations.description: Na máquina de puxada de costas, segure a barra com as mãos relativamente próximas. Use uma pegada pronada (P.ex.: o dorso das suas mãos vão ficar voltados para a máquina). Então puxe a barra em uma linha reta, como se fosse em direção ao chão. Garanta que seus bíceps sejam a força motriz do movimento. Você provavelmente sentirá seus ombros e latíssimo trabalhando, mas garanta que seus bíceps sejam os músculos mais afetados.

translations.name: Isometria trazioni impugnatura inversa
translations.description: Trazioni in isometria con impugnatura inversa

translations.name: Incline Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
translations.description:
Set up an adjustable bench at a 45-degree angle.
    Lay on your stomach with your head hanging just above the edge of the bench.
    Grab a dumbbell in each hand and set up with a good posture – core and lats engaged and shoulders neutral.
    Row the dumbbells toward the top of the stomach and squeeze the back at the top of the rep.
    Finally, lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat until all reps are completed.

translations.name: Pseudo Planche Push-up
translations.description: You should have the shoulder line in front of your wrists an perform a push-up, maintaining the shoulder in the same position

translations.name: Talons fesses
translations.description: Touch your heels to your buttocks, while remaining static or moving

translations.name: Dynamic Planche
translations.description:
1. Start in a plank position, with your hands directly under your shoulders and your feet hip-width apart.
  2. Engage your core to keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
  3. From this position, bend your knees slightly and jump with both feet inward, bringing them as close to your core as possible
  4. As soon as your feet touch the ground, push off forcefully to jump again and return to the starting plank position.
  5. Continue repeating this jumping motion, maintaining control and core stability throughout the exercise.
  6. Make sure to keep your breathing steady while jumping, breathing deeply and controlling your breathing.
  7. Keep your gaze fixed on the ground to maintain proper spinal alignment.
  8. Repeat the jumps for the desired length of time or the number of repetitions recommended for your workout program.
  9. To finish the exercise, rest by releasing the plank position, then stretch if necessary to loosen your muscles.

translations.name: Planche dynamique
translations.description: 1. Commencez en position de planche, avec vos mains directement sous vos épaules et vos pieds écartés à la largeur des hanches. 2. Contractez vos abdominaux pour maintenir votre corps en ligne droite de la tête aux pieds. 3. À partir de cette position, pliez légèrement les genoux et sautez avec les deux pieds vers l'intérieur, en les rapprochant de votre tronc autant que possible. 4. Dès que vos pieds touchent le sol, poussez avec force pour sauter à nouveau et revenir à la position de planche initiale. 5. Continuez à répéter ce mouvement de saut, en gardant le contrôle et en maintenant la stabilité de votre tronc tout au long de l'exercice. 6. Assurez-vous de garder une respiration régulière pendant que vous sautez, en respirant profondément et en contrôlant votre respiration. 7. Gardez votre regard fixé sur le sol pour maintenir un bon alignement de la colonne vertébrale. 8. Répétez les sauts pendant la durée souhaitée ou selon le nombre de répétitions recommandé pour votre programme d'entraînement. 9. Pour terminer l'exercice, reposez-vous en relâchant la position de planche, puis étirez-vous si nécessaire pour détendre vos muscles.

translations.name: Reach ups
translations.description: Pour effectuer cet exercice, allongez-vous sur le dos, bras étendus au-dessus de la tête. Ensuite, contractez vos abdominaux et soulevez votre torse du sol en tendant les bras vers le plafond pour "atteindre" le plus haut possible. Revenez lentement à la position de départ en contrôlant le mouvement. Les reach ups renforcent les muscles abdominaux et améliorent la flexibilité de la colonne vertébrale. Ils peuvent être intégrés à une variété de routines d'entraînement pour travailler la force et la stabilité du tronc.

translations.name: Planche de cote dynamique
translations.description: La planche latérale dynamique implique de se placer sur le côté, en appui sur un coude et le côté du pied, puis de basculer les hanches de haut en bas tout en maintenant une ligne droite du corps.

translations.name: Curl con mancuernas sentado
translations.description: Sujeta dos pesas, los brazos estirados, las manos a los lados, las palmas hacia dentro. Flexiona los brazos y sube el peso con un movimiento rápido. Al mismo tiempo, gira los brazos 90 grados al principio del movimiento. En el punto más alto, gira un poco las pesas hacia fuera. Sin pausa, bájalas lentamente.No permitas que tu cuerpo se balancee durante el ejercicio, todo el trabajo lo realizan los bíceps, que son los únicos músculos que deben moverse (presta atención a los codos).

translations.name: Seated Dumbbell Curls
translations.description: Seated Dumbbell Curls are an effective bicep workout that isolates the muscles by stabilizing the upper body, reducing momentum that can detract from the exercise’s effectiveness. This exercise is performed sitting down with a dumbbell in each hand, focusing on controlled movement to maximize engagement of the bicep muscles.

translations.name: Reverse Grip Barbell Curls
translations.description:
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing down) approximately shoulder-width apart. Ensure your back is straight, shoulders slightly pulled back, and arms fully extended.
Curl the barbell upwards towards your chest in a controlled motion, keeping your elbows close to your body. The motion should be smooth without any swinging or momentum use.
Once the barbell is at chest level, pause briefly to maximize contraction in the biceps and forearms.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with a controlled movement, fully extending your arms.

translations.name: Trazioni impugnatura inversa
translations.description: Trazioni alla sbarra con i i pollici rivolti verso l'esterno

translations.name: One armed push-ups
translations.description: Perform push-ups with one hand, alternating the sides

translations.name: Piegamenti a una mano
translations.description: Piegamenti sulle braccia eseguito con una sola mano

translations.name: Arco femorale una gamba
translations.description: Nello specifico i muscoli coinvolti sono: bicipite femorale. muscolo semimembranoso. muscolo semitendinoso.

translations.name: Barbell Bench Press - NB
translations.description: The bench press is a compound exercise that builds strength and muscle in the chest and triceps. When many people think of listing, the bench press is often the first exercise that comes to mind

translations.name: Low-Cable Cross-Over - NB
translations.description: The low-cable cross-over is an isolation movement that uses a cable stack to target the upper portion of the pectoral muscles. It is common in upper-body and chest-focused muscle-building workouts, often in combination with presses or flyes from other angles to target all portions of the chest.

notes.comment: With a slight bend in your arms, draw your hands upward and toward the midline of your body. Your hands should come together in front of your chest, palms facing up.
To move into the starting position, place the pulleys at the low position, select the resistance to be used and grasp a handle in each hand.
Return your arms back to the starting position after a brief pause.
Step forward, gaining tension in the pulleys. Your palms should be facing forward, hands below the waist, and your arms straight. This will be your starting position.

translations.name: EZ-Bar Skullcrusher - NB
translations.description: The EZ-bar skullcrusher is a popular exercise targeting the triceps muscles. The EZ-bar is used more often than a straight bar. As for the name, that’s worst-case scenario. The bar should actually come down behind the head. It is usually performed for moderate to high reps as part of an upper body or arms-focused workout. No need to chase a 1RM on this move!

notes.comment: Keeping the upper arms stationary, lower the bar by allowing the elbows to flex. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Pause once the bar is directly above the forehead.
Lift the bar back to the starting position by extending the elbow and exhaling.
Using a close grip, lift the EZ bar and hold it with your elbows in as you lie on the bench. Your arms should be perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

translations.name: High-Cable Cross Tricep Extention - NB
translations.description:
The high-cable cross tricep extension is an isolation exercise for targeting the triceps,
 particularly the long head. It utilizes cables and handles grasped with opposite hands, allowing for a unique pressing motion that can be effective for building tricep strength. Unlike exercises that use a straight bar or rope, this variation can be more comfortable on your wrists and elbows

translations.name: Preacher Curl - Internally Rotated
translations.description: Single arm curl that can be done using a dumbbell and a preacher bench, or a preacher curl machine. Turn body towards the weight

translations.name: Lat Pulldown - Cross Body Single Arm
translations.description: Sit at lat pulldown machine with body at a diagonal angle so that only one leg is under the pad. Pull the cable down with the opposite arm. this will force the cable to across your body while pulling down. Emphasises the stretch of the lat.

translations.name: Cable Chest Press - Incline
translations.description: Single arm chest press done with the cable machine. Use the other arm to brace bodyweight to focus on strength of the press, rather than balancing of the body. Start with the hand as close to the chest as possible, and then press against the cable at a slight incline and aiming towards the center of your chest.

translations.name: JM Press
translations.description: The JM press keeps your shoulders stationary, relying on the elbow flexion and extension to move the weight using your triceps strength. That means you get all the benefits of the best triceps-strengthening exercises without overtaxing that delicate shoulder joint. Classic win-win!

translations.name: Helms Row
translations.description:
Setup: Place your chest on the end of a bench, using a towel or anything soft to keep your chest protected. You want your chest to be in contact with the bench around the nipple line. Your back should be almost parallel to the floor

Execution: When you’re starting the exercise, focus on pulling your elbows back until you feel maximum tightness in the lats.

If you want to get some trap activation with the exercise, relax your shoulder blades when the weights are hanging down and then retract them as you start the rep.

notes.comment: If you want to focus on lat activation, keep your scapula (shoulder blades) engaged and tight.

translations.name: Meadows Row
translations.description: The Meadows row is a unilateral row performed with a landmine setup, overhand grip, and staggered stance. Lean your torso forward and grip the barbell. Rest the other forearm on the forward leg. Start this movement by driving the elbow behind you while retracting the shoulder-blade. Keep the working shoulder down. Pull toward your back hip until the elbow is level with your torso.

notes.comment: Use reduced diameter plates to increase range of motion. Avoid bigger plates.

translations.name: Cable Triceps Press
translations.description: A single arm exercise that starts with the hand close to the chest with with the elbows bent, and the elbow flared out. Press forward and down against the cable straightening the elbow.

translations.name: Cable Tri Extension - Internal Rotation
translations.description: single arm exercise with cable held at opposite shoulder with elbow bent. Turn towards the cable, and the straighten the elbow across the body.

translations.name: Front Plank
translations.description:
The plank is a bodyweight exercise. As a multi-functional movement, the plank not only targets your abdominal muscles but also the spine and hip. Plank strengthens and tightens your entire body, improves your posture and balance, reduces body fat, and can help boost your metabolism.
Exercises such as the “plank pose” help strengthen the stamina of stabilizing abdominal muscles. It can also help relieve back pain associated with a weakening of the function of the stabilizing muscles of the body.
Planks are a versatile exercise that targets many of the most important muscle groups in the body, so they can be applied by anyone to improve endurance and overall body strength.

translations.name: Cable Tri Extension - External Rotation
translations.description: single arm exercise with cable held at opposite shoulder with elbow bent. Turn away from the cable, and the straighten the elbow across the body.

translations.name: Curl  - With Shoulder Elevated
translations.description: Place elbow so that it is about level with the shoulder. Curl the wieght to your shoulder. Focus on the stretch in the eccentric part of the curl

translations.name: Bodyweight Squat HD
translations.description:
Squat is a type of bodyweight exercise. It is one of the most popular exercises for strength and muscle growth. Squat is particularly effective for focusing on the muscles of the leg and hips.
Squat are an easy exercise for beginners to do. It can help strengthen leg muscles, tighten hip muscles and burn calories to lose weight.
It tightens the butt and legs. Squats are very effective for firming and strengthening your legs by acting on the gluteus,hip flexors, quadriceps, hamstrings and inner thigh muscles. Also, bodyweight squats can help shape your glutes and butt.

notes.comment: 2.- Flexiona lentamente las rodillas y deja caer las caderas para bajar el cuerpo. Mantén los talones apoyados en el suelo.
4.- Repite el número de repeticiones deseado
3.- Al final del ejercicio, haz una pausa y sube con fuerza hasta la posición inicial, imitando la bajada.

translations.name: Bodyweight Squat HD
translations.description:
La sentadilla es un tipo de ejercicio de peso corporal. Es uno de los ejercicios más populares para la fuerza y el crecimiento muscular. La sentadilla es particularmente efectiva para enfocarse en los músculos de la pierna y las caderas.
Las sentadillas son un ejercicio fácil de hacer para los principiantes. Puede ayudar a fortalecer los músculos de las piernas, tensar los músculos de la cadera y quemar calorías para perder peso.
Aprieta los glúteos y las piernas. Las sentadillas son muy efectivas para reafirmar y fortalecer las piernas al actuar sobre los glúteos, los flexores de la cadera, los cuádriceps, los isquiotibiales y los músculos internos del muslo. Además, las sentadillas con peso corporal pueden ayudar a dar forma a los glúteos.

translations.name: Lying Leg Raise
translations.description: Leg raise is one of the most effective exercises for the lower abdominal muscles. Although this exercise targets the rectus abdominus and oblique muscles, it is particularly useful for burning and tightening the lower fat.

notes.comment: 1- Lie on your back with your arms placed by your sides. Raise your legs into a vertical position, keeping your knees and feet together and your back and hips neutral.
2- Slowly lower your legs, keeping your feet and knees together, controlling the movement with your core and keeping your torso stationary.

translations.name: Jumping Jack HD
translations.description:
Jumping jack are a plyometric exercise. Plyometrics are explosive aerobic moves that increase speed, quickness, and power and they work your whole body.
Jumping jack target the arm, shoulder, abdominal muscles, hip muscles and hip flexors and also work on the thighs, knee tendons and quadriceps.
Jumps are beneficial to your health because they combine cardiovascular conditioning with strength work. Since jumps elevate your heart rate, they can also improve your cardiovascular fitness.

notes.comment: 1.- Ponte de pie con las piernas juntas y los brazos a los lados.
3.- Mientras saltas, separa las piernas a la altura de los hombros. Estira los brazos por encima de la cabeza.
2.- Dobla ligeramente las rodillas y salta en el aire.
4.- Vuelve a la posición inicial.

translations.name: Jumping Jack HD
translations.description:
Los saltos de tijera son un ejercicio pliométrico. Los pliométricos son movimientos aeróbicos explosivos que aumentan la velocidad, la rapidez y la potencia y trabajan todo el cuerpo.
El salto de tijera se enfoca en el brazo, el hombro, los músculos abdominales, los músculos de la cadera y los flexores de la cadera y también trabaja en los muslos, los tendones de la rodilla y los cuádriceps.

Los saltos son beneficiosos para la salud porque combinan el acondicionamiento cardiovascular con el trabajo de fuerza. 

Dado que los saltos elevan el ritmo cardíaco, también pueden mejorar la condición cardiovascular.

notes.comment: 2.- Dobla ligeramente las rodillas y salta en el aire.
4.- Vuelve a la posición inicial.
3.- Mientras saltas, separa las piernas a la altura de los hombros. Estira los brazos por encima de la cabeza.

translations.name: Bodyweight Squat HD
translations.description:
Squat is a type of bodyweight exercise. It is one of the most popular exercises for strength and muscle growth. 
Squat is particularly effective for focusing on the muscles of the leg and hips.

Squat are an easy exercise for beginners to do. It can help strengthen leg muscles, tighten hip muscles and burn calories to lose weight.

It tightens the butt and legs. Squats are very effective for firming and strengthening your legs by acting on the gluteus,hip flexors, quadriceps, hamstrings and inner thigh muscles. 

Also, bodyweight squats can help shape your glutes and butt.

notes.comment: 1.- Coloca los pies separados a la altura de los hombros, con los dedos ligeramente girados hacia fuera. Contrae los abdominales inferiores y mantén la mirada al frente.
3.- Al final del ejercicio, haz una pausa y sube con fuerza hasta la posición inicial, imitando la bajada.
2.- Flexiona lentamente las rodillas y deja caer las caderas para bajar el cuerpo. Mantén los talones apoyados en el suelo.
4.- Repite el número de repeticiones deseado.

Jumping jack target the arm, shoulder, abdominal muscles, hip muscles and hip flexors and also work on the thighs, knee tendons and quadriceps.

Jumps are beneficial to your health because they combine cardiovascular conditioning with strength work. Since jumps elevate your heart rate, they can also improve your cardiovascular fitness.

notes.comment: 1.- Stand with your legs together and your arms at your sides.
3.- While jumping, spread your legs apart at shoulder height. Stretch your arms above your head.
2.- Bend your knees slightly and jump in the air.
4.- Return to the starting position.

translations.name: Salto de Tijera HD
translations.description:
Los saltos de tijera son un ejercicio pliométrico. Los pliométricos son movimientos aeróbicos explosivos que aumentan la velocidad, la rapidez y la potencia y trabajan todo el cuerpo.

El salto de tijera se enfoca en el brazo, el hombro, los músculos abdominales, los músculos de la cadera y los flexores de la cadera y también trabaja en los muslos, los tendones de la rodilla y los cuádriceps.

Los saltos son beneficiosos para la salud porque combinan el acondicionamiento cardiovascular con el trabajo de fuerza. Dado que los saltos elevan el ritmo cardíaco, también pueden mejorar la condición cardiovascular.

notes.comment: 1.- Ponte de pie con las piernas juntas y los brazos a los lados.
3.- Mientras saltas, separa las piernas a la altura de los hombros. Estira los brazos por encima de la cabeza.
2.- Dobla ligeramente las rodillas y salta en el aire.
4.- Vuelve a la posición inicial.

translations.name: Plancha HD
translations.description: Una plancha es un ejercicio isométrico de fuerza central que consiste en mantener una posición similar a una flexión de brazos durante el máximo tiempo posible. Se puede incorporar de manera efectiva en regímenes generales de acondicionamiento físico, programas de entrenamiento atlético de alto rendimiento e incluso protocolos de rehabilitación física.

translations.name: Plank
translations.description: A plank is an isometric core strength exercise that involves maintaining a position similar to a push-up for the maximum possible time. It can be effectively incorporated into general fitness regimens, high-performance athletic training programs, and even physical rehabilitation protocols.

notes.comment: 1- Lie face down on an exercise mat with your elbows to your sides, your head facing forward, and palms flat on the floor.

translations.name: High Knee Skips HD
translations.description:
You can use this exercise both as a dynamic warm-up before training and add it to your cardio training routine to burn fat.

High knee skips are a plyometric exercise. Plyometrics are explosive aerobic moves that increase speed, quickness, and power and they work your whole body.

High knee skips target the oblique, leg muscles, hip muscles and hip flexors and also work on the thighs, knee tendons, quadriceps and shoulders.

Jumps are beneficial to your health because they combine cardiovascular conditioning with strength work. Since jumps elevate your heart rate, they can also improve your cardiovascular fitness.

notes.comment: 1.- Stand upright with your feet apart at waist height.
3.- Change to lift your right knee to your chest.
2.- Lift your left knee to your chest.
4.- Continue the movement, alternating legs and moving at a sprinting or running rhythm.

translations.name: Saltos Altos De Rodilla HD
translations.description:
Puedes utilizar este ejercicio tanto como calentamiento dinámico antes de entrenar como añadirlo a tu rutina de entrenamiento cardiovascular para quemar grasa.

Los saltos altos de rodilla son un ejercicio pliométrico. Los pliométricos son movimientos aeróbicos explosivos que aumentan la velocidad, la rapidez y la potencia y trabajan todo el cuerpo.

Los saltos altos de rodilla se dirigen a los oblicuos, los músculos de las piernas, los músculos de la cadera y los flexores de la cadera y también trabajan en los muslos, los tendones de la rodilla, los cuádriceps y los hombros.

Los saltos son beneficiosos para la salud porque combinan el acondicionamiento cardiovascular con el trabajo de fuerza. Dado que los saltos elevan el ritmo cardíaco, también pueden mejorar la condición cardiovascular.

notes.comment: 1.- Colócate de pie con los pies separados a la altura de la cintura.
3.- Cambia para levantar la rodilla derecha hasta el pecho.
2.- Levanta la rodilla izquierda hacia el pecho.
4.- Continúa el movimiento, alternando las piernas y moviéndote a un ritmo de sprint o carrera.

translations.name: Abdominales HD
translations.description:
El movimiento crunch es uno de los ejercicios más básicos diseñados para fortalecer los músculos centrales del cuerpo. El ejercicio ayuda a fortalecer los músculos centrales, mejorar la postura y aumentar la movilidad y flexibilidad muscular.
Mejora los músculos del six pack: Cuando se realiza el ejercicio de abdominales, los músculos rectos abdominales y oblicuos se tensan, por lo que se desarrollan los músculos abdominales superiores y los músculos del six pack.
Aumenta la fuerza de los músculos abdominales: La función principal de los músculos abdominales es estabilizar la sección media. Te apoya mientras levantas objetos pesados, lo que te permite girar y rotar tu cuerpo. Estas son acciones de todo el día que no notas, por lo que es importante que tus músculos abdominales puedan soportar largas horas de trabajo. El ejercicio crunch ayuda a desarrollar esta importante resistencia en los músculos abdominales. La resistencia muscular es la capacidad de estas fibras para resistir la resistencia durante mucho tiempo.

notes.comment: 1.- Túmbate boca arriba con las rodillas flexionadas y los pies apoyados en el suelo, separados a la anchura de las caderas.
2.- Coloca las manos detrás de la cabeza de forma que los pulgares queden detrás de las orejas.
3.- No juntes los dedos.
4.- Mantén los codos extendidos hacia los lados pero ligeramente redondeados hacia dentro.
5.- Inclina ligeramente la barbilla, dejando unos centímetros de espacio entre la barbilla y el pecho.
6.- Tire suavemente de los abdominales hacia dentro.
7.- Inclínese hacia arriba y hacia delante de modo que la cabeza, el cuello y los hombros se levanten del suelo.
8.- Aguanta un momento en la parte superior del movimiento y vuelve a bajar lentamente.

translations.name: Crunches HD
translations.description:
Crunch movement is one of the most basic exercises designed to strengthen the core muscles of the body. Exercise helps to strengthen core muscles, improve posture, and increase muscle mobility and flexibility.
Improves six pack muscles: When crunch exercise is done, the rectus abdominus and oblique muscles are tightened, so the upper abdominal muscles and six pack muscles develop.
Increases the strength of the abdominal muscles: The primary role of your abdominal muscles is to stabilize your mid-section. It supports you while lifting heavy objects, allowing you to twist and rotate your body. These are all day long actions that you do not notice, so it is important that your abdominal muscles can sustain long hours of work. Crunch exercise helps build this important endurance in the abdominal muscles. Muscular endurance is the ability of these fibers to resist resistance for a long time.

notes.comment: 1.- Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
2.- Place your hands behind your head so that your thumbs are behind your ears.
4.- Keep your elbows extended out to the sides but slightly rounded inwards.
5.- Slightly tilt your chin, leaving a few centimeters of space between your chin and chest.
6.- Gently pull the abdominals inward.
7.- Lean upward and forward so that the head, neck and shoulders are lifted off the floor.
8.- Hold for a moment at the top of the movement and slowly lower back down.
3.- Do not clasp your fingers together.

translations.name: Bench Dips On Floor HD
translations.description:
Triceps dips on floor are a compound exercise as they worked multiple muscle groups simultaneously. Although this bodyweight exercise mainly targets the triceps, it also hits your chest and front of your shoulder.
Triceps dips on floor are one of the most effective exercises to increase arm strength and also build lean muscle in your upper arms.
Triceps dips on floor are a closed kinetic chain exercise and express that you do the movements around a fixed point. It increases compression force on your joints thereby improving stability.

notes.comment: 1.- Take the parallel bars and jump upwards, stretch your arms.
2.- Lower your body by bending your arms while leaning forward until your shoulders are below your elbows.
3.- Lift your body stretching your arms. Lock your elbows at the top.

translations.name: Inmersiones De Banco En El Piso HD
translations.description:
Los fondos de tríceps en el suelo son un ejercicio compuesto, ya que trabajan varios grupos musculares simultáneamente. Aunque este ejercicio de peso corporal se enfoca principalmente en los tríceps, también golpea el pecho y la parte delantera del hombro.
Los fondos de tríceps en el suelo son uno de los ejercicios más efectivos para aumentar la fuerza de los brazos y también desarrollar músculo magro en la parte superior de los brazos.
Los fondos de tríceps en el suelo son un ejercicio de cadena cinética cerrada y expresan que haces los movimientos alrededor de un punto fijo. Aumenta la fuerza de compresión en las articulaciones, mejorando así la estabilidad.

notes.comment: 1.- Coge las barras paralelas y salta hacia arriba, estira los brazos.
2.- Baja el cuerpo doblando los brazos mientras te inclinas hacia delante hasta que los hombros queden por debajo de los codos.
3.- Levanta el cuerpo estirando los brazos. Bloquea los codos en la parte superior.

translations.name: Kopfüber Gewichtaufheben
translations.description:
Diese Maschine neben Beinheben.
Kopf nach vorne, mit dem gesicht zum boden und das Gewicht aufheben

translations.name: Bodyweight lunge HD
translations.description:
Bodyweight lunges are an effective calisthenic exercise for 
strengthening the lower body, improving balance and stability, and 
developing functional strength. They are a popular choice for bodyweight workouts, home workouts, and can also be included as part of a larger strength training routine.

translations.name: Lateral Push Off
translations.description: Push off the ground and land on one leg and regain balance before jumping to the other leg

translations.name: Extension Triceps Poulie Basse à Un Bras
translations.description:
Réglez la poulie au bas de la machine à câble et saisissez-la **sans utiliser d'accessoire**. **Étendez le câble directement au-dessus de votre tête.

Tout en gardant votre dos droit et votre bras supérieur immobile, abaissez le câble derrière votre tête jusqu'à ce que vous sentiez un bon étirement dans vos triceps, puis étendez-le à nouveau vers le haut jusqu'à ce que votre **coude soit verrouillé (ou complètement tendu).

translations.name: One Arm Overhead Cable Tricep Extension
translations.description:
Set the pulley at the bottom of the cable machine and grab onto it without using any attachments. Extend the cable directly overhead.

While keeping your back straight and upper arm stationary, lower the cable behind your head until you feel a good stretch in your triceps, and then extend it back upward until your elbow is locked out.

translations.name: Jumping Jacks
translations.description: A full-body exercise where you jump while spreading your legs and raising your arms overhead.

translations.name: Polichinelos
translations.description: Exercício de corpo inteiro onde você salta abrindo as pernas e levantando os braços acima da cabeça.

translations.name: Círculos de perna (frente-trás)
translations.description: Fique em pé ao lado de uma parede ou apoio estável. Balance uma perna para frente e para trás em movimento controlado, mantendo o tronco ereto e o abdômen contraído. Troque de perna após completar as repetições.

translations.name: Leg Swings (Front–Back)
translations.description: Stand tall next to a wall or stable support. Swing one leg forward and backward in a controlled motion, keeping the torso upright and the core engaged. Alternate legs after completing the repetitions.

translations.name: Seated Hip Abduction
translations.description:
Starting Position:
  • Adjust the seat height so that your knees are aligned with the pivot point of the machine.
  • Sit with your back flat against the backrest, maintaining good posture.
  • Place the outside of your thighs against the machine's padded levers.
  • Position your feet flat on the footrests or platform of the machine.
  • Grasp the handles or sides of the seat for stability.
  • Ensure your spine is neutral and your core is slightly engaged.
Movement:
  • Exhale as you slowly push your legs outward against the resistance pads.
  • Focus on initiating the movement from your hips, not your knees.
  • Continue opening your legs until you feel a strong contraction in your outer hips and thighs.
  • Hold the fully abducted position briefly (1-2 seconds) to maximize muscle engagement.
  • Inhale as you slowly control the return of your legs to the starting position, resisting the weight throughout the movement.
  • Avoid allowing the weight stack to touch down between repetitions to maintain constant tension on the muscles.
  • Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining control throughout the set.

translations.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlifts
translations.description:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell with both hands.
  2. With your eyes facing forward, lift the dumbbells and place them at pelvic level (At this time, do not bend your arm).
  3. With your back straight, bend your upper body and slowly lower the dumbbells to knee height.
  4. Lift the dumbbells in the reverse order and return back to position on step 2.

translations.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
translations.description:
1. Sit on the floor holding a dumbbell in each hand, knees bent and feet flat.
  2. Roll back and plant your upper back and head on the floor; keep hips on the ground.
  3. Hold dumbbells at chest level with palms facing each other (neutral) or facing forward; elbows tucked at about 45–75° to the torso.
  4. Brace your core and press both dumbbells upward until arms are nearly extended but not locked.
  5. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells under control until your upper arms contact the floor (or just above it) — this shortens the range of motion.
  6. Repeat for desired reps.
    Coaching notes: keep shoulders packed, avoid arching hips, use a spotter or lighter weight if needed. The floor press reduces shoulder stress and emphasizes lockout strength.

translations.name: Forearm Curls (underhand grip)
translations.description:
• Muskulatur anspannen
  • Kein Schwung holen
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Bewegung

translations.name: Push-Ups
translations.description:
• 1. Hands shoulder-width on the floor, fingers forward; feet together or hip-width; body in a straight line from head to heels; engage core and glutes.
  • 2. Inhale and bend elbows, keeping them at ~45° from the torso (or closer for triceps focus); lower chest toward the floor until elbows reach ~90° or chest nearly touches.
  • 3. Briefly hold at the bottom with tension in chest and core.
  • 4. Exhale and push through palms to extend elbows, returning to the starting plank position without locking elbows.

  • Muskulatur anspannen
  • Kein Schwung holen
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Bewegung
  • Immer angewinkelt lassen
  • Nicht überdehnen

translations.description: Lie on a flat bench and press dumbbells upward until arms are fully extended, then lower them slowly.

translations.description: Deite-se em um banco plano e empurre os halteres para cima até que os braços estejam totalmente estendidos, depois abaixe-os lentamente.

translations.name: Wall Angels
translations.description: The Wall Angels exercise primarily targets the upper back and shoulder muscles, helping improve posture, shoulder mobility, and scapular control.

translations.name: seated figure four
translations.description: Seat on  a bench. Feet flat on the ground. Place your left ankle over your right knee. Your right knee stays flat. Keep your back straight. Gently push your left knee down.

translations.name: Zottman curl
translations.description: With your palms facing forward, curl the weights up to your shoulders. Turn your hands so that that your palms downwards and slowly return the weights back down. Finally face your palms back forward.

translations.name: Lower Back Extensions
translations.description:
• Muskulatur anspannen
  • Hintern ein wenig anheben
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Bewegung
  • Kein Schwung holen
  • Oberkörper gerade halten

translations.name: Lat Pull Down (close grip)
translations.description:
• Muskulatur anspannen
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Bewegung
  • Oberkörper aufrecht halten
  • Zur Brust ziehen

translations.name: Thruster
translations.description:
1. Start by doing a front squat
  2. At the top position, push the bar above your head (similar to a press)
  3. Lower the bar to the shoulders

translations.name: Dumbbell Thruster
translations.description:
Start with the dumbbells resting on your shoulders and squat down. Push 
into standing and raise the dumbbells into an overhead position. Bring 
the dumbbells back to your shoulders and repeat.

translations.name: Dumbbell Hex Press
translations.description:

  • Kontrollierte Bewegung

translations.name: Glute Bridge Single-Arm Press
translations.description: With one dumbbell in hand, perform a glute bridge. Now hold the dumbbell above your chest, this is the rep starting position. Lower the dumbbell so that your elbow touches the floor or is roughly 45 degrees below your shoulder if using a bench. Push the dumbbell back into the starting position.

translations.name: Bear crawl pull through
translations.description:
Place a dumbbell at around hip level and assume a bear crawl position. Push the knees off the floor and hold.
With the opposite hand reach for the dumbbell and pull it to the other side. Place your hand back on the floor and repeat with your other hand.

translations.name: Double Kettlebell Clean and Press
translations.description: Full-body muscles building exercise. This exercise provides a huge range of benefits in terms of strength & size and is extremely functional.

translations.name: Double Kettlebell Front Squat
translations.description: The kettlebell front squat is a compound, multi-joint exercise that works several muscle groups.

translations.name: Single Leg RDL
translations.description: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, shift your weight onto one leg and slightly bend the other. Keep your back straight while hinging at your hips. Use your hamstring to pull yourself back up.

notes.comment: Maintain a straight back and hinge from the hips

translations.name: box jumps
translations.description: Jump from a standing position onto the box, stretch your body, then step down again (do not jump)

translations.name: Bauch-Twist Gerät
translations.description:
- Langsam und stätig
- Nicht zu viel Gewicht nutzen

translations.name: Rotary Torso Machine
translations.description:
- Slow and Steady
- Not too much weight

images.license_title: Girl doing one arm shoulder raise

translations.name: Cable Lateral Raises (Single Arm)
translations.description:
The single arm cable lateral raise is a variation of the lateral raise and an exercise used to build the muscles of the shoulders.
  1. Position a cable at the lowest position possible and attach a single handle.
  2. Reach across your body and grab the handle with a neutral grip.
  3. Keep the elbow slightly bent and pull the handle across your body and raise laterally.
  4. Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position under control.

notes.comment: Keep the abs braced and don’t arch the back at the top of the movement
Focus during the movement and don’t rely on momentum
Allow the arms to move freely but don’t lock out the elbows.
Tilt your pinkies slightly higher than your thumbs. Imagine you’re pouring out a cup of water.

translations.name: Recumbent Bike
translations.description: For this exercise Recumbent Bike is needed. You just sit on it, set level and time and start the workout

translations.name: TORSO TWIST
translations.description: Standing Torso Twist WorkoutTarget your core and improve flexibility with this dynamic standing exercise! The Standing Torso Twist Workout involves twisting your torso while keeping your feet and hips stable, engaging your obliques, and stretching your entire upper body. This movement helps to:Strengthen core musclesIncrease flexibility in the spine and torsoImprove posture and balanceEnhance overall athletic performancePerform 3 sets of 10-15 reps, twisting to each side, to feel the benefits of this effective and efficient workout!

translations.name: Plancha Lateral HD
translations.description:
La plancha lateral es un entrenamiento eficaz para fortalecer el tronco. Puede ayudarlo a fortalecer y desarrollar los músculos de la parte superior e inferior del cuerpo.
Al ser un movimiento multifuncional, este ejercicio se enfoca no solo en los músculos abdominales, sino también en la columna vertebral y los glúteos. La plancha fortalece y tensa todo el cuerpo, mejora la postura y el equilibrio, reduce la grasa corporal y puede ayudar a acelerar el metabolismo.

notes.comment: 1.- Acuéstese sobre su lado derecho, elevándose sobre su antebrazo derecho.
2.- Coloca el hombro derecho directamente sobre el codo derecho, manteniendo los hombros, las caderas y las rodillas en línea recta.
3.- Apoya el brazo izquierdo a lo largo del costado del cuerpo.
4.- Antes de presionar, contrae los abdominales, aleja los hombros de las orejas y alarga la columna vertebral.

translations.name: Side Plank
translations.description:
Side plank are an effective core strengthening workout. It can help you strengthen and develop both upper and lower body muscles.
Being a multi-functional movement, this exercise targets not only your abdominal muscles but also the spine and buttocks. Plank strengthens and tightens your entire body, improves your posture and balance, reduces body fat and can help speed up your metabolism.

notes.comment: 1.- Lie on your right side, raising yourself onto your right forearm.
2.- Place your right shoulder directly above your right elbow, keeping your shoulders, hips and knees in one straight line.
3.- Rest your left arm along the side of your body.
4.- Before you press up, draw your abs in, drop your shoulders away from your ears, and lengthen your spine.
5.- Push up so that you body is straight… Hold for 10 seconds for beginner to 1 Minute if more advanced. Repeat on your left side.

translations.name: Band pull-aparts
translations.description:
• Hold the band with your arms extended in front of you at shoulder height.
  • Keep your elbows slightly bent but fixed throughout the movement.
  • Pull the band apart by spreading your arms until the band touches or comes close to your chest.
  • Slowly bring your arms back to the starting position, controlling the tension on the band.

translations.name: Pull-apart avec élastique
translations.description:
• Tiens l'élastique avec les bras tendus devant toi, à hauteur des épaules.
  • Garde les coudes légèrement fléchis, mais fixes.
  • Tire l'élastique vers l'extérieur en écartant les bras jusqu'à ce qu'il touche ou approche ta poitrine.
  • Ramène lentement les bras à la position initiale en contrôlant la tension.

translations.name: Upper Back
translations.description: Upper Back eignet sich für den Aufbau der Rumpfmuskultur mit besonderem Schwerpunkt auf den Delta- und Rautenmuskel und der oberen Rückenmuskulatur

translations.name: Upper Back
translations.description: Upper Back is suitable for building up the core muscles with a special focus on the deltoid and rhomboid muscles and the upper back muscles

translations.name: Pullover Machine
translations.description: Pullover machine, sitting, elbows on pads

translations.name: Hamstring Kicks
translations.description: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and extend one arm in front of you. Swing that leg up in front of you and back down while keeping your leg as straight as possible and your toes pointed up.Repeat with the other leg.

notes.comment: Keep your upper body upright and stable
Focus on controlling the movement

translations.name: Hamstring-Kicks
translations.description: Stehen Sie mit hüftbreit geöffneten Füßen. Schwingen Sie ein Bein vor und zurück, wobei Sie es so gerade wie möglich halten und die Zehen nach oben zeigen. Wiederholen Sie dies mit dem anderen Bein.

notes.comment: Halten Sie Ihren Oberkörper stabil und benutzen Sie bei Bedarf Ihre Arme zur Balance

translations.name: Single Leg RDL
translations.description:
1. Stand upright and hold weights in both hands if using loads.
  2. Brace your core and lift one leg off the ground.
  3. Keep your back straight, hinge at the hips while lowering your torso forward, ensuring you don’t rotate your hips.
  4. Lower until you feel a stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to standing position. Repeat on both sides.

notes.comment: Maintain balance and focus on proper form to prevent injury.

translations.name: Toe Touch
translations.description: Stand with your feet closer together. Hinge at your hips and lower your upper body towards your toes. Reach your hands towards your feet, try to touch your toes, the ground, or as far down your legs as you can comfortably go.

notes.comment: Keep your legs straight but don't lock your knees

translations.name: Elephant Walks
translations.description: Hinge at your hips until you feel a stretch behind your knees. Bend one leg while the other is straight then fluidly bend the other knee while straightening the first knee.

notes.comment: Maintain a fluid motion between leg bends

translations.name: Good Morning
translations.description: Stand with your feet more than shoulder-width apart (wider stance) and place your hands on your hips. Hinge at your hips and lower your upper body towards the ground while keeping your back straight then come back up.

notes.comment: Keep your back straight throughout the movement

translations.name: Single Leg Hamstring Stretch
translations.description: Sit on the ground with one leg straight out in front of you and the other leg bent in toward you. Reach forward with both hands, trying to touch the toes of your straight leg.

notes.comment: Keep the extended leg straight but don't lock the knee

translations.name: Sit & Reach
translations.description: Sit on the ground with your legs extended straight in front of you. Reach both hands forward, trying to reach past your toes or as far as you can go. Return to the starting position and repeat.

notes.comment: Keep your legs straight but don't lock your knees

translations.name: Crossbody Leg Swings
translations.description: Hold onto a wall or something near you for support. Swing one leg across the front of your body, then back and out to the side. Repeat while slowly increasing your range of motion.

notes.comment: Keep your supporting leg slightly bent for stability

translations.name: Standing Pancake
translations.description: Stand and open your legs as wide as possible into a straddle position. Then bend at the hips, pushing them back while keeping your back straight.

notes.comment: Keep your back straight as you bend forward

translations.name: Hamstring Chokes
translations.description: Sit on the ground and bend one leg, bringing your knee toward you. Place your hand behind your hamstring and gently pull your leg toward your chest, feeling a stretch in the back of your thigh. Straighten your leg, extending up toward the ceiling.

notes.comment: Maintain control throughout the movement, especially when extending the leg

translations.name: Roll Down
translations.description: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and slowly roll your upper body forward, one vertebra at a time, until your hands touch the ground or as far as you can comfortably go. Roll back up to the starting position in the same controlled manner.

notes.comment: Move slowly and deliberately, focusing on each vertebra as you roll down and up

translations.name: Crossbody Hamstring Stretch
translations.description: While standing, cross one leg behind the other (keep this leg straight). Reach downward and try to touch the toes of that leg. Slightly bend the other leg so that you can bend down.

notes.comment: Keep the crossed leg straight to maximize the hamstring stretch

translations.name: Preacher Curl - Externally Rotated
translations.description: Single arm curl that can be done using a dumbbell and a preacher bench, or a preacher curl machine. Turn body away from the weight

translations.name: Kickstand RDL
translations.description: use non-working leg's toes to help with balance and perform an RDL.

translations.name: Estensione Plank-Gomito
translations.description:
Esecuzione

  1. Posizione di partenza :

    • Parti in posizione di plancia alta (mani appoggiate a terra, braccia completamente estese, corpo in linea retta dalla testa ai talloni).
    • Piedi leggermente divaricati per maggiore stabilità.

  2. Movimento :

    • Piega un gomito e appoggia l'avambraccio a terra, seguito dall'altro gomito, passando così alla posizione di plank sugli avambracci.
    • Torna alla posizione iniziale spingendoti verso l'alto con un braccio alla volta, estendendo completamente i gomiti.

  3. Respirazione :

    • Espira mentre spingi verso l'alto ed estendi le braccia.
    • Inspira mentre scendi sugli avambracci.

translations.name: Plank-to-Elbow Extension
translations.description: The Plank-to-Elbow Extension is a dynamic exercise that combines the plank with an elbow extension movement

translations.name: Cossack squat
translations.description: The Cossack Squat is a multi-joint exercise that works mainly on the legs and buttocks, but also involves stabilizing muscles. It is an excellent exercise for improving strength, mobility and stability in a functional way.

translations.name: Wall-sit
translations.description: The Wall Sit (or wall chair) is an isometric exercise that mainly involves the leg muscles, improving muscular endurance and stability. Here are the main and secondary muscles activated during the exercise.

translations.name: Dragon-flag
translations.description:
Keep your body completely rigid throughout the movement, avoiding sagging in your lower back.
Use a firm grip to stabilize your upper body.
Start with simpler versions (such as with bent knees) to build strength and control.

translations.name: Plancha con elevación de pierna
translations.description: En posición de plancha, levanta una pierna alternadamente.

translations.name: Plank with Alternating Leg Lift
translations.description: In a plank position, lift one leg alternately.

translations.name: Heel Touches
translations.description:
• Starting Position:

    • Lie on your back on an exercise mat.
    • Bend your knees, keeping your feet flat on the floor and hip-width apart.
    • Place your arms along your sides, palms facing inward, just off the floor.

  • Engage Core:

    • Lift your head, shoulders, and upper back slightly off the ground.
    • Keep your neck neutral and chin slightly tucked.

  • Perform the Movement:

    • Side-bend to the right, reaching your right hand toward your right heel.
    • Return to the center, then side-bend to the left, reaching your left hand toward your left heel.
    • Continue alternating sides in a controlled manner.

  • Breathing:

    • Exhale as you reach toward your heel and inhale as you return to the center.

translations.name: Toques de Talón
translations.description:
• Posición inicial:

    • Acuéstate de espaldas sobre una esterilla de ejercicio.
    • Flexiona las rodillas, manteniendo los pies planos en el suelo y separados al ancho de las caderas.
    • Coloca los brazos a lo largo de los costados, con las palmas hacia adentro, apenas despegados del suelo.

  • Activa el core:

    • Eleva ligeramente la cabeza, los hombros y la parte superior de la espalda del suelo.
    • Mantén el cuello en una posición neutral y la barbilla ligeramente hacia adentro.

  • Realiza el movimiento:

    • Inclínate hacia la derecha, llevando tu mano derecha hacia tu talón derecho.
    • Vuelve al centro y luego inclínate hacia la izquierda, llevando tu mano izquierda hacia tu talón izquierdo.
    • Continúa alternando lados de manera controlada.

  • Respiración:

    • Exhala al alcanzar el talón e inhala al regresar al centro.

translations.name: Abdominales en bicicleta
translations.description: Los abdominales en bicicleta son un ejercicio para el core que se realiza en el suelo. Acuéstate de espaldas con las manos detrás de la cabeza y las piernas extendidas. Eleva los hombros del suelo, lleva una rodilla hacia el pecho y gira el torso para que el codo opuesto toque la rodilla. Alterna los lados en un movimiento de pedaleo, asegurándote de mantener el core comprometido durante todo el ejercicio.

translations.name: bicycle crunches
translations.description: Bicycle crunches are a core exercise performed on the floor. Lie on your back with your hands behind your head and legs extended. Lift your shoulders off the ground, bring one knee toward your chest, and twist your torso so your opposite elbow meets the knee. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion, ensuring controlled movements and engaging your core throughout the exercise.

translations.name: Hack Squats
translations.description: Hack squats target the lower body and are performed on a hack squat machine or with a barbell. For the machine variation, position yourself on the platform with your shoulders under the pads and feet slightly forward. Push through your heels to lift the weight, then bend your knees to lower the platform in a controlled motion until your thighs are parallel to the ground. Push back up to the starting position. For the barbell variation, hold the bar behind your legs with your arms extended and perform a squat-like motion.

translations.name: Sentadillas Hack
translations.description: Las sentadillas hack son un ejercicio para la parte inferior del cuerpo que se realiza en una máquina hack squat o con barra. En la variación con máquina, coloca los hombros bajo las almohadillas y los pies ligeramente hacia adelante en la plataforma. Empuja con los talones para levantar el peso, luego flexiona las rodillas para bajar la plataforma de manera controlada hasta que los muslos estén paralelos al suelo. Empuja de vuelta a la posición inicial. En la variación con barra, sostiene la barra detrás de las piernas con los brazos extendidos y realiza un movimiento similar a una sentadilla.

translations.name: Abdominales con mancuerna
translations.description: Los abdominales con mancuerna son una variación con peso de los abdominales tradicionales, diseñados para trabajar los músculos abdominales con mayor intensidad. Acuéstate de espaldas con las rodillas dobladas y los pies apoyados en el suelo. Sostén una mancuerna con ambas manos cerca del pecho o sobre la cabeza. Levanta los hombros y la parte superior de la espalda del suelo en un movimiento de contracción, activando el core. Baja lentamente a la posición inicial. Asegúrate de mantener movimientos controlados durante el ejercicio para evitar lesiones.

translations.name: Dumbbell Crunches
translations.description: Dumbbell crunches are a weighted variation of traditional crunches designed to target the abdominal muscles more intensely. Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell with both hands close to your chest or above your head. Lift your shoulders and upper back off the ground in a crunching motion, engaging your core. Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position. Ensure controlled movements throughout the exercise to prevent strain.

translations.name: Biceps Curl Machine
translations.description: The biceps curl machine is designed to isolate the biceps muscles and provide a controlled range of motion. Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and adjust the seat so your arms are aligned with the machine's handles or pads. Grip the handles firmly, keeping your elbows fixed in place, and curl the handles upward by contracting your biceps. Slowly lower the handles to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.

translations.name: Curl de bíceps sentado
translations.description: La máquina de curl de bíceps está diseñada para aislar los músculos del bíceps y proporcionar un rango de movimiento controlado. Siéntate en la máquina con la espalda apoyada en el respaldo y ajusta el asiento para que tus brazos estén alineados con las manijas o almohadillas de la máquina. Agarra las manijas con firmeza, manteniendo los codos fijos en su lugar, y levanta las manijas contrayendo los bíceps. Baja las manijas lentamente a la posición inicial, manteniendo el control durante todo el movimiento.

translations.name: Toe Taps
translations.description: Toe taps are a core-focused exercise performed lying on your back. Lie flat with your arms by your sides and your legs raised to a tabletop position (knees bent at 90 degrees). Slowly lower one foot to gently tap the floor, keeping your core engaged and your lower back pressed into the ground. Return to the starting position and alternate legs. This exercise strengthens the core while minimizing strain on the lower back.

translations.name: Toques de pie alternados
translations.description: Los toques de pie son un ejercicio enfocado en el core que se realiza acostado de espaldas. Acuéstate con los brazos a los lados y las piernas elevadas en posición de mesa (rodillas dobladas a 90 grados). Baja lentamente un pie para tocar el suelo suavemente, manteniendo el core activado y la espalda baja presionada contra el suelo. Regresa a la posición inicial y alterna las piernas. Este ejercicio fortalece el core mientras reduce la tensión en la parte baja de la espalda.

translations.name: Crunch lateral de pie
translations.description: Los crunches laterales de pie son un ejercicio dinámico que trabaja los oblicuos mientras mejora el equilibrio y la estabilidad. Párate derecho con los pies a la altura de los hombros y las manos detrás de la cabeza o sosteniendo una mancuerna en una mano. Inclina el torso hacia un lado, contrayendo los oblicuos, mientras llevas el codo hacia la cadera (o realizas el crunch hacia el peso si usas mancuerna). Regresa a la posición inicial y alterna lados o realiza todas las repeticiones en un lado antes de cambiar.

translations.name: Standing Side Crunches
translations.description: Standing side crunches are a dynamic exercise that targets the obliques while improving balance and stability. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and hands behind your head or holding a dumbbell in one hand. Lean your torso to one side, contracting your obliques, while bringing your elbow toward your hip (or crunching toward the weight if using a dumbbell). Return to the starting position and alternate sides or perform all repetitions on one side before switching.

translations.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Rotations
translations.description:
Dumbbell shoulder rotations are designed to strengthen the rotator cuff muscles and improve shoulder stability and mobility.

  1. External Rotation: Hold a dumbbell in one hand, keeping your elbow bent at 90 degrees and close to your body. Rotate your forearm outward, away from your body, keeping your elbow stationary. Slowly return to the starting position.
  2. Internal Rotation: Hold a dumbbell in one hand, keeping your elbow bent at 90 degrees and close to your body. Rotate your forearm inward, toward your body, and slowly return to the starting position. Perform the exercise with controlled movements to avoid strain.

translations.name: Rotaciones de hombro con mancuerna
translations.description:
Las rotaciones de hombro con mancuerna están diseñadas para fortalecer los músculos del manguito rotador y mejorar la estabilidad y movilidad del hombro.

  1. Rotación Externa: Sostén una mancuerna en una mano, con el codo doblado a 90 grados y pegado al cuerpo. Rota el antebrazo hacia afuera, alejándolo del cuerpo, manteniendo el codo fijo. Vuelve lentamente a la posición inicial.
  2. Rotación Interna: Sostén una mancuerna en una mano, con el codo doblado a 90 grados y pegado al cuerpo. Rota el antebrazo hacia adentro, acercándolo al cuerpo, y vuelve lentamente a la posición inicial. Realiza el ejercicio con movimientos controlados para evitar lesiones.

translations.name: Plate Pinch Hold
translations.description: The plate pinch hold is a grip strength exercise designed to target the forearms and improve grip endurance. Select one or two weight plates (smooth-edged plates work best) and pinch them together between your thumb and fingers. Hold the plates with your arm straight down by your side or extended slightly in front of you. Maintain the hold for as long as you can, keeping your shoulders relaxed and your grip firm. This exercise can be done for time or repetitions.

translations.name: Sostén con pellizco de disco
translations.description: El sostén con pellizco de disco es un ejercicio para mejorar la fuerza de agarre y la resistencia en los antebrazos. Selecciona uno o dos discos de pesas (los discos de bordes lisos son ideales) y pínzalos entre el pulgar y los dedos.
