Mantén los discos con el brazo recto hacia abajo a tu lado o ligeramente extendido frente a ti. Sostén la posición el mayor tiempo posible, manteniendo los hombros relajados y el agarre firme. Este ejercicio puede realizarse por tiempo o repeticiones.

translations.name: Reverse Wood Chops
translations.description:
• Attach a looping resistance band to a rack below knee height
  • Grip the other end of the band like holding a baseball bat with both hands
  • Stretch the band facing it
  • Start with your hands at the side of your hips, knees bent
  • Rotate your hips while moving your hands towards over the top of your opposite shoulder
  • Imagine the motion as striking a baseball with a bat

notes.comment: Keep arms almost fully extended throughout
Use as much force as possible

translations.name: Lat Pull DB
translations.description:
• Bend upper body forward
  • Move dumbbells near your body with slightly bent arms towards your back

translations.name: Scapula Pulls
translations.description:
• Hang straight on a pull-up bar
  • Pull shoulder blades together, moving the body slightly up

translations.name: Pin Bench Press BB
translations.description:
• Set security pins to about the height of your sticking point
  • Lower the bar, rest on the pins for 1s while holding tension
  • Move bar up with maximum force

translations.name: Pin Squat
translations.description:
• Set security pins to about the height of your sticking point
  • Lower the bar, rest on the pins for 2s keeping tension
  • Stand up with maximum force

translations.name: Clean
translations.description:
Regular olympic lift clean.
Pull bar from ground, catch on shoulders performing a front squat.

notes.comment: Keep ellbows up

translations.name: Pin OHP
translations.description:
• Set security pins to the height of your lower chin
  • Rest bar on pins keeping tension
  • Raise bar over head

translations.name: Push OHP
translations.description:
• Rest bar in front on your shoulders
  • Bend knees while keeping upper body straight
  • Extend legs pushing the bar overhead with force

translations.name: Incline OHP DB
translations.description:
• Sit on a bench with 45° incline
  • Ellbows 45° out
  • Move dumbbells down until upper arms are parallel to shoulders
  • Move dumbbells up, meeting overhead

translations.name: Kreis Press DB
translations.description:
• Sit on a bench with 45° incline
  • Hold dumbbells overhead
  • Move dumbbells down as if doing overhead press
  • Rotate dumbbells palms facing up and extend your arms in front of your body
  • Move hands together until dumbbells meet, keep arms extended
  • Reverse the motion until dumbbells are overhead again
  • This is one repetition

translations.name: Shoulder Raise Side and Front DB
translations.description:
• Stand while holding dumbbells beside body
  • Keep arms almost fully extended
  • Without momentum raise dumbbells to the side of your body until shoulder height, palms facing down
  • Lower dumbbells beside body, palms facing inwards
  • Immediately raise dumbbells in front of your body until shoulder height, palms facing down
  • Down again
  • This is one repetition

translations.name: Incline Chest Press DB
translations.description:
• Bench 45° incline
  • Hold dumbbells parallel on your chest
  • Press up until arms extended

translations.name: Jerk OL
translations.description:
Olympic lift jerk
  • Hold bar in front on shoulders like push press
  • Bend and extend knees pushing bar up while diving under the bar
  • Catch bar overhead with straight arms using lunge
  • Move feet parallel while holding bar in lockout

translations.name: Clean and Jerk OL
translations.description:
Olympic lift clean and jerk.
Combination of clean and jerk in one motion.

translations.name: Snatch OL
translations.description:
Olympic lift snatch
  • Pull barbell from ground to overhead lockout using a wide grip and overhead squat.
  • Move bar slowly until cleared knees then explosively extend hips, pull bar up and dive under the bar
  • Catch bar overhead with straight arms
  • Push head forward during lockout

translations.name: Curl inclinado con mancuernas
translations.description: Sentado en un banco con el respaldo inclinado en un ángulo de 40 a 60 grados. Deja que tus brazos cuelguen hacia el suelo. Sujeta una mancuerna en cada mano en supinación.

translations.name: Curl incliné avec haltères
translations.description: Asseyez-vous sur un banc incliné à 60∘. Tenez les haltères le long du corps près du sol, paumes vers l'extérieur, bras complètement étendus. Soulevez les haltères en utilisant uniquement les biceps et sans élan (sans donner d'à-coups).

translations.name: Seated W Curl
translations.description:
• Sit on bench with 60° incline
  • Hold dumbbells beside body near the ground, palms facing outwards, arms fully extended
  • Move dumbbells up using only biceps and without momentum

translations.name: ClimbMill
translations.description: The ClimbMill, also known as a stair climber, is a cardio-focused machine that simulates climbing stairs. It provides an effective way to improve endurance while strengthening the lower body. To use the machine, step onto the moving stairs, maintain an upright posture, and use the handrails for balance if needed. Adjust the speed and intensity to match your fitness level. This exercise helps improve cardiovascular health, burns calories, and enhances lower body muscle endurance.

translations.name: Escaladora
translations.description: La ClimbMill, también conocida como escaladora o máquina de subir escaleras, es una máquina de cardio que simula la acción de subir escaleras. Es una excelente forma de mejorar la resistencia y fortalecer la parte inferior del cuerpo. Para usarla, sube a los escalones en movimiento, mantén una postura erguida y usa las barras de apoyo solo si es necesario. Ajusta la velocidad e intensidad según tu nivel de condición física. Este ejercicio mejora la salud cardiovascular, quema calorías y aumenta la resistencia muscular.

translations.name: Cobra Stretch
translations.description: The Cobra Stretch is a back extension exercise that helps improve spinal flexibility and relieve lower back tension. To perform, lie face down on the floor with your hands placed under your shoulders. Press your palms into the floor and lift your chest while keeping your hips on the ground. Keep your elbows slightly bent and your shoulders relaxed. Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly lower yourself back down. This exercise is beneficial for improving posture and reducing lower back stiffness.

translations.name: Postura de la Cobra (en Yoga)
translations.description: El estiramiento de Cobra es un ejercicio de extensión de espalda que ayuda a mejorar la flexibilidad de la columna y aliviar la tensión en la zona lumbar. Para realizarlo, acuéstate boca abajo con las manos apoyadas debajo de los hombros. Presiona las palmas contra el suelo y levanta el pecho mientras mantienes las caderas en el suelo. Mantén los codos ligeramente flexionados y los hombros relajados. Sostén la posición por unos segundos y luego baja lentamente. Este ejercicio es ideal para mejorar la postura y reducir la rigidez en la parte baja de la espalda.

translations.name: Estiramiento de rotación del torso
translations.description:
El estiramiento de rotación del torso mejora la movilidad de la columna y alivia la tensión en la espalda baja y los oblicuos. Puede realizarse de pie o sentado.

  1. Variante de pie: Párate derecho con los pies a la altura de los hombros. Coloca las manos en las caderas o extiende los brazos al frente. Gira lentamente el torso hacia un lado, manteniendo las caderas estables. Mantén la posición unos segundos y luego gira hacia el otro lado.
  2. Variante sentada: Siéntate en una silla con los pies planos en el suelo. Coloca una mano en el exterior del muslo contrario y gira suavemente el torso hacia ese lado. Mantén el estiramiento antes de cambiar de lado.

translations.name: Torso rotation strech
translations.description:
The torso rotation stretch helps improve spinal mobility and relieves tension in the lower back and obliques. It can be performed standing or seated.

  1. Standing Variation: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart. Place your hands on your hips or extend your arms in front of you. Slowly rotate your torso to one side, keeping your hips stable. Hold for a few seconds, then rotate to the opposite side.
  2. Seated Variation: Sit on a chair with your feet flat on the floor. Place one hand on the outside of your opposite thigh and gently twist your torso toward that side. Hold the stretch before switching sides.

translations.name: Estiramiento de rodilla al pecho
translations.description: El estiramiento de rodilla al pecho es un ejercicio simple pero efectivo para aliviar la tensión en la zona lumbar y mejorar la movilidad de la cadera. Para realizarlo, acuéstate de espaldas con las piernas extendidas. Lleva una rodilla hacia el pecho y abraza la pierna con ambas manos, sujetando la espinilla o la parte posterior del muslo. Tira suavemente de la rodilla hacia el pecho mientras mantienes la otra pierna estirada en el suelo. Mantén el estiramiento durante 15–30 segundos y luego cambia de lado. Para un estiramiento más profundo, puedes llevar ambas rodillas al pecho al mismo tiempo.

translations.name: Knee to Chest Stretch
translations.description: The knee to chest stretch is a simple yet effective exercise for relieving lower back tension and improving hip mobility. To perform, lie flat on your back with your legs extended. Bring one knee toward your chest, wrapping your hands around your shin or behind your thigh. Gently pull the knee closer to your chest while keeping the other leg straight on the floor. Hold the stretch for 15–30 seconds, then switch sides. For a deeper stretch, both knees can be pulled toward the chest simultaneously.

translations.name: Towel Superman
translations.description:
In this exercise, you lie flat on your stomach, hold a 
towel with your arms extended in front of you, and pull it apart to 
create tension. Then, you move it forward and back under your stomach. 
This exercise targets the back and shoulder muscles as well as core 
stability.

translations.name: Cable Press Around
translations.description:
Set the cable pulley at chest height and attach a D-handle bar. Grab the handle with a neutral grip and hold it next to your chest with your elbow fully flexed and tight to your side. Turn away from the pulley so your torso is at 45 degrees.

Hold the other side of the functional trainer with your corresponding hand. Assume a staggered stance for better balance.

While keeping your chest proud, extend your elbow at 45 degrees across your midline while keeping your arm parallel to the floor. Stop shy of lockout and contract your chest at the top of the range of motion (ROM).

translations.name: Cross-Body Cable Y-Raise
translations.description: Attach a D-Bar to the cable machine. Perform a motion akin to drawing a sword from across your body.

translations.name: No Leg Drive Dumbbell Chest Press
translations.description: Set up for a normal Dumbbell chest press but lift your leg ups straight or put them on another bench to remove the leg drive.

translations.name: punches
translations.description: stand stable and throw normal straight punches

translations.name: Dumbbell Underhand Dead Row
translations.description:
The Dumbbell Underhand Dead Row will involve the back, which means you can try a bit heavier weight. The catch is that you need to be able to control the weight for all the reps.
  1. Start by holding a pair of dumbbells with your feet shoulder-wide apart and your knees slightly bent.
  2. Hinge at your hips to lower your torse forward until it's almost parallel to the floor, keeping your back flat and maintaining a slight bend in your knees.
  3. Exhale as you row the dumbbells to your sides up to chest height, leading with your elbows until your upper arms are just past parallel to the floor and the dumbbells are at ribcage level.
  4. Slowly lower to the starting position and repeat. Keep your back flat all the time.

translations.name: Pause Hack Squats
translations.description: Hack Squats but with a 1-2s pause at the bottom of the movement. This makes sure that there is no more elastic energy stored in the muscles.

translations.name: Curl Araña
translations.description:
1. Comience colocando la barra en la parte del banco en la que normalmente se sentaría. Debe asegurarse de alinear la barra correctamente para que no se caiga y esté equilibrada.
  2. Muévase a la parte delantera del banco, la parte donde generalmente se encuentran los brazos, y colóquese para recostarse en un ángulo de 45 grados con el estómago y el torso presionados contra la parte delantera del banco.
  3. Los pies, y especialmente los dedos de los pies, deben estar bien colocados en el piso. Luego coloque los brazos superiores sobre la almohadilla que se encuentra en la parte interior del banco. La almohadilla superior debe estar ajustada en las axilas.
  4. Use sus brazos para agarrar la barra con las palmas hacia arriba, con un poco de ancho de hombro o un poco más cerca. Su agarre para este ejercicio será importante. Sosteniendo las manos juntas, apuntará a la cabeza externa del bíceps, mientras que cuanto más separadas estén las manos, apuntará a la cabeza interna. Puede experimentar haciendo un conjunto de rizos con un agarre más cercano y otro conjunto con un agarre más ancho para asegurarse de golpear ambas cabezas del bíceps.
  5. Poco a poco comience a levantar la barra hacia arriba y exhale. Asegúrese de mantener la posición contratada por un segundo y luego apriete los bíceps. Al hacer esto, desea mantener la parte superior de los brazos estacionaria y rizar el peso lo más alto posible.
  6. Vuelva a colocar la barra lentamente en la posición inicial y asegúrese de respirar.
  7. Haga una breve pausa antes de repetir la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones para su set.

translations.name: Spider Curl
translations.description:
To get the most out of DB Spider Curls, you need an adjustable weight bench set at a 45-degree incline. This inclined position allows your arms to hang freely, putting them in an extended start position with elbows slightly in front of your torso, which is the key to maintaining low-level isometric tension throughout the movement pattern.

  1. Lie face-down on an adjustable weight bench with your chest fully supported, holding a pair of dumbbells.
  2. Keep a neutral head position and don’t crane your neck.
  3. Let your arms hang straight down with your palms facing forward (for a standard Spider Curl) or palms facing inward for a Hammer Curl exercise variation.
  4. Keep a stable foot position on the floor to maintain balance and control.
  5. Start the curl motion by flexing your biceps muscles and lifting the pair of dumbbells or curl bar toward your shoulders.
  6. Keep your elbow flexion controlled. Your arms should stay locked in position with no unnecessary movement.
  7. Squeeze hard at the top for 1-2 seconds. This is where maximum tension hits the biceps muscle fibers.
  8. Lower the weight under control for 3-4 seconds to keep muscles under tension for the entire range of motion.

notes.comment: Perform 3-4 sets with 10-12 reps, maintaining strict form and avoiding any swinging or momentum.

translations.name: Calf Raise using Hack Squat Machine
translations.description: Ideally using a trapeze addon, Lift the weight up using your calves by getting on your toes.

translations.name: Incline Close Grip Barbell Bench Press
translations.description: Narrower grip than regular bench press, just outside shoulder width.

translations.name: Floor Skull Crusher
translations.description: Allowing you to safely load more weight than a regular skull crusher, lie on the floor and rest the weight on the floor in between each repetition.

translations.name: Bent over Cable Flye
translations.description: Holding your upper body at an approximate 105° angle.

translations.name: 1-Arm Half-Kneeling Lat Pulldown
translations.description: Attach a D-Handle to a high pully. And use your lat muscles to pull the weight single handedly.

translations.name: Kroc Row
translations.description: Dumbbell rows with looser technique but heavier weight.

translations.name: Cable Shrug-In
translations.description: Attach two D-Handles to two low cables and shrug in using your upper traps.

translations.name: Reverse Cable Flye
translations.description: Attach D-Handles to two cable pulleys in the upper position. Grab the left on with your right hand and vice-versa.

translations.name: W-Raise
translations.description:
This challenging exercise is a variation of reverse crunches that is made up of three parts in which we’re basically drawing an upside down ‘W’ with our legs.

Start position is lying face up flat on the floor with legs extended at the low point of the outer leg of the ‘W’. Keeping a strong core and legs straight, go up, rise your hips and then slowly lower your legs down halfway. Then, lifting your legs back up to the top, rise your hips again, maintaining that straight line, use your core strength to finally come back all the way down to the other outer leg of the W. Then you reverse the ‘W’ to return to the start.

translations.name: Black Widow Knee Slides
translations.description:
In
this bottom-up rotation exercise movement, you’ll cross your knee over and
drive it into that opposite elbow. Start in high plank position (or tabletop
position) with hands directly beneath your shoulders. Lift the left knee toward
the right arm and slide it up the forearm to get more of that posterior pelvic
tilt and engagement of the abdominal muscles. Then do the opposite side,
bringing your right knee toward your left hand.

translations.name: Butterfly Sit Up
translations.description:
In
this midrange exercise your arm muscles provide you with a little bit of
momentum to help to get you off the ground. You also open them up in overhead
position which engages the upper back. Start
lying with feet flat on the ground, knees at an angle, arms crossed in towards your chest. Using
your ab muscles, bring your upper body off the ground as you open your arms
into goal post position, then slowly lower yourself back down to return to
starting position.

notes.comment: Be sure to pull your belly button in toward your spine throughout this crunch variation.

translations.name: Seated Corkscrew
translations.description:
To
do this abs and obliques exercise, start with your hands back behind your body
and bring your knees in and across, really trying to contract the obliques.
Then extend your legs back out to starting position and repeat toward the
opposite side of the abs.

Beginners
might find that they can’t even get through the first 45 seconds of this
challenging reverse crunches variation.  That’s ok because it gives you a
place to start and something to improve upon.

notes.comment: Engage your core as you move upper and lower body at the same time.
Make sure your belly button is pulled in toward the spine throughout the entire motion.

translations.name: Levitation Crunch
translations.description:
For
this top down movement, we’re trying to move the upper torso without moving the
lower torso. Start lying on the ground with feet flat on the floor and crossed
arms above your head with hands behind head. Lift upper body up and clear your
shoulder blades off the ground and then hold and pause at the top for a one or
two count. Try to make the upper abs work and hold that contraction for 10 good
quality reps.

notes.comment: Make sure your belly button is pulled in toward the spine throughout the entire motion.

translations.name: Sit Up Elbow Thrust
translations.description:
This
is a top down rotation movement, and it’s a bit more
explosive, too.

Lying with knees bent and feet on the floor, sit up and then drive your left elbow across your entire body toward the right, then come back
to center and then finally lower yourself down. Then hit the other side.

notes.comment: Make sure your belly button is pulled in toward the spine throughout the entire motion.

translations.name: Sit-Ups (Ellenbogen zum Knie)
translations.description:
• Füße fixieren
  • Muskulatur anspannen
  • Kein Schwung holen
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Durchführung
  • Gleichmäßig atmen

translations.name: Lying Triceps Extensions
translations.description:
1. Starting position for this practical triceps exercise is lying on a standard bench holding a barbell or two dumbbells in both hands with an overhand grip, hands at shoulder width apart.
  2. Begin with arms over your upper chest and elbows bent back at about a 45-degree angle.
  3. First bend at the elbows and then allow the upper arm to drop back, bringing the barbell/dumbbells behind your head and down toward the floor. In terms of upper arm position, the barbell/dumbbells should never be fully above your head, but instead behind it, to ensure that you’re targeting the triceps.
  4. Keep the shoulder blades tucked under, the elbows tight in toward your head and your core active during this entire movement.

translations.name: Drag Pushdown
translations.description:
1. Starting position for this rope pushdown is standing facing a cable machine with the handles of a rope attachment in both hands and feet shoulder width apart.
  2. Hinge forward slightly at the hips maintaining an upright chest and bring the shoulders and elbows behind the body so that when you push down on the cable attachments, you can get a fully contracted triceps long head.
  3. Drag the cable machine rope attachment as close to the body as possible and straighten your elbows until lockout.

translations.name: Dumbbell Cheat Curl
translations.description:
1. Starting position is standing with feet shoulder width apart.
  2. Hands should take an underhand or supinated grip on the dumbbells holding them with hands shoulder width apart.
  3. Keeping elbows tucked into your sides throughout the entire movement, use momentum to curl the dumbbells, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement.
  4. Slowly lower to return to the starting position. Keep the core tight throughout the exercise.
Cheating through the concentric curling portion of this challenging exercise gives us a great opportunity to increase time under tension and create eccentric overload with heavier weight when we lower.

translations.name: Bizeps Curls Trifecta
translations.description:
This exercise is a mixture of three different curls: the Supinated Cross Body Curl, the Pronated Cross Body Curl and the No Money Curl. You alternate between three curl variations that each accomplish different goals, and you’ll keep the set going beyond the usual 8-12 rep range which gives us that intensity.

  1. For the Supinated Cross Body Curl, supinate the forearm with palms facing toward the ceiling and forearms coming across the body. Lift the inner-facing weight of the dumbbell towards your shoulder. 
  2. For the Pronated Cross Body Curl, pronate the forearm with palms facing toward the floor (pronated grip or overhand grip) and forearms coming across the body. Lift the inner-facing weight of the dumbbell towards your shoulder. 
  3. For the No Money Curl, you’ll curl the dumbbell as you outwardly rotate the shoulder. Lift the inner-facing weight of the dumbbell towards your outer side of the shoulder.

translations.name: Omni Cable Cross-over
translations.description:
Adjust the weights based on the motion. When pulling from top to bottom, increase the weight by 20% to 40% more than the bottom-to-top motion. This accounts for the fact that when operating from top down, the upper chest muscles are naturally weaker than the lower chest muscles. So, adjust your weight stack to compensate.
  1. Position one cable at a high setting and the other at the lowest level setting. Get into a staggered stance in between the cable towers.
  2. For the cable set low, pull it upward and across your body. This effectively targets the upper chest.
  3. For the cable set high, draw it downward and across your body. This will primarily engage the lower chest.
Once you’re done, swap the positions of the cables. Change the previously high cable to the lowest position and vice versa. Repeat the exercise sequence, ensuring you’re targeting both chest regions effectively.

translations.name: Rocking Triceps Pushdown
translations.description: Set your feet below the rope and lean your upper body towards the rope. Set one foot back. Push down the rope and lean your entire body back onto the rear foot as you push down to get your hands behind your body. Don't forget to bring your chest to the front during the pushdown. As you release the rope slowly, lean your body back onto the front foot.

notes.comment: Don't use your body weight to pull the rope down. Let the triceps do all the work.
The back&forth movement of your body is purely to keep a more perpendicular position between arm and rope.

translations.name: Alternating High Cable Row
translations.description:
1. Start by putting the attachment in a high position on the cable station and step back about 1m from the attachment. Grab the cable pulley handle in your left hand at about head height with your elbow slightly bent. Step back with your left foot.
  2. Pull your elbow joint in toward the torso twisting slightly and perform a single-arm row. Engage the lats as you twist.
  3. Return to the starting position with cable pulley and left foot and grab the cable pulley handle in the right hand. Repeat this motion on the opposite side with your right foot stepped back.

notes.comment: Use your core strength for stability and keep spine stable for all repetitions.
Focus on taking the lats through their entire range of motion in this cable pulley movement.

translations.name: Hyper Y W Combo
translations.description:
1. Use a glute-ham raise or a stability ball for this exercise and a light weight plate in each hand.
  2. Raise your torso to form a straight line with your legs and raise the arms outward slightly beyond a 90-degree angle into a W position to hit the rotator cuff muscles, and then lower back to the starting position.
  3. Then raise up again with arms in a Y position to activate the lower traps.

translations.name: Dumbbell Pullover
translations.description:
1. Starting position is lying across a flat bench perpendicularly with your feet flat on the ground, holding a dumbbell overhead with both hands.
  2. With a slight bend in the elbow, begin lowering the dumbbell over and behind the head with the arm muscles in extended position.
  3. Then reverse the movement bringing the dumbbell back up and over the head.
  4. Keep your abdominal muscles tight and spine stable throughout allow repetitions and don’t allow your back to arch up away from the flat bench.

notes.comment: This is an excellent dumbbell exercise for laser targeting the lats.

translations.name: Plank Jacks
translations.description: Jumping jacks from the plank position.

translations.name: Lying Triceps Kickback
translations.description: Set the bench to an 60deg angle. Stand still and rest your upper body on the inclined bench, ensuring a nearly straight line of your entire body. Squeeze your shoulder blades together, so that your elbows are located behind your torso with your upper arms nearly parallel to the floor. Lift the dumbbells until your arms form a straight line. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the initial position. Initial position corresponds to an rectangular position of upper and lower arms.

notes.comment: Keep your upper arms more or less parallel to the floor during the entire exercise to maximize muscle activation.
Start with some light weights to focus on a proper form and a good mind-muscle connection.

translations.name: Remada unilateral no cabo
translations.description: pegada neutra A remada unilateral, também conhecida como remada serrote, é um exercício que fortalece as costas e os bíceps, além de melhorar a postura.

translations.name: High Row
translations.description: Kneeling down on one leg and pulling cable down while driving elbow into the lats.

translations.name: Bayesian Curl
translations.description: Cable curl with stretched shoulder (backwards)

translations.name: Sitting Calf Raises
translations.description: Calf raises while sitting using extra weight on the platform.

translations.name: Push-Up
translations.description:
The push-up is a fundamental bodyweight exercise that targets the chest, arms, and shoulders while engaging the core for stability. It requires no equipment and is excellent for building upper body strength.

Instructions:

  1. Start in a plank position with hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width, feet together, and body in a straight line from head to heels.
  2. Engage your core and lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body or flared slightly outward.
  3. Lower until your chest nearly touches the ground while maintaining a neutral spine.
  4. Push through your palms to return to the starting position, fully extending your arms.

translations.name: Push-Up
DB Upper Chest Variation
translations.description: Dumbbell in hand in a curl fashion, lean body into the arm to help the dumbbell up above the chest, next to the head, to activate chest and delt combo

translations.name: DB Underhand bench press
translations.description: Underhand grip DB, perform a normal bench press movement, keeping the elbows close to the chest

translations.name: Elbows Tucked DB Bench Press
translations.description: Elbows Tucked DB Bench Press, chest press movement focusing on the triceps. DB stays parallel with body

translations.name: DB Skull Crushers
translations.description: Alike tricpes extensions, DBs fall down near the head and pushed back up. Unlike for the triceps extension tring to extend the arms as far back

translations.name: DB UCV
translations.description: Dumbbell upper chest variance. Curl like movement up above at head level, activating chest and front delts

translations.name: Alternative DB Gorilla rows
translations.description: Slightly bent down and knees to mimic a gorilla position and pull/row up the DBs to the chest. DB stay straight (thumbs pointing up)

translations.name: DB Cross Body Hammer Curls
translations.description: Dumbbell cross body biceps curls, works on the braccialis

translations.name: Dumbbell Frog Press
translations.description: Similar to hip thrust, but with feet put up together, lifting the butt off the ground in a frog like position.

translations.name: Dumbbel Bradford press
translations.description: From a front hold of the DBs in a OHP, press above the head, bring back towards the rear of the shoulders, down, and press back forward.

translations.name: Alternating dumbbell hammer curl
translations.description: Stand with your knees slightly bent and your back straight. Hold a dumbbell in each hand, using a neutral grip at your sides.

translations.name: Curl alterno de martillo con mancuernas
translations.description: De pie, con las rodillas ligeramente dobladas y la espalda recta. Sujeta una mancuerna en cada mano, en un agarre neutral a lo largo del cuerpo.

translations.name: Hammercurl mit Kurzhanteln, abwechselnd
translations.description: Stehen Sie mit leicht gebeugten Knien und geradem Rücken. Halten Sie in jeder Hand eine Hantel und verwenden Sie einen neutralen Griff an Ihren Seiten.

translations.name: Cable Fly Middle Chest
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Start position: Arms extended out to your sides (a big “T” shape), feeling a stretch in your chest.

  2. Movement: Bring your hands together in front of your chest in a wide hugging motion.

    • Focus on squeezing your chest at the center.

    • Do not lock your elbows or turn it into a press.

  3. Return: Slowly open your arms back to the start, maintaining tension on the chest.

translations.name: Cable Fly Upper Chest
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Start position:

    • Arms down and slightly behind your body, elbows slightly bent.

    • Feel a light stretch across your chest.

  2. Movement:

    • Bring your hands upward and together in front of your upper chest — roughly at chin to collarbone level.

    • Use a smooth, controlled motion (avoid jerking).

    • Squeeze your chest at the top for 1–2 seconds.

  3. Return:

    • Slowly let your arms move back down and out to the sides, keeping control and tension.

translations.name: High-Incline Smith Machine Press
translations.description: Set the bench to a 45-60° incline. Touch the upper chest with the bar.

translations.name: Cable Tricep Kickback
translations.description:
Grab onto the handle, and pull your elbow up and back slightly above 
your torso. Keep your upper arm to be parallel to the ground for the 
duration of the set

translations.name: Neutral Grip Lat Pulldown
translations.description: Lat pull downs with a neutral grip on the bar.

translations.name: Kong Curl
translations.description:
This exercise is somewhat similar to a cable curl and a cable crossover. But instead of a chest/biceps exercise, this exercise targets the brachialis muscle, responsible for that broad arm when viewed from a front view.
During the exercise, maintain a pronated forearm position with starting position of approx. 45 degree pronated. Start with slightly bend elbows and alternately curl until your hands (your thumb first) meet your upper middle chest (similar to how Kong hits his chest, thus the name of this exercise).

translations.name: Drop Curl
translations.description:
Drop curls in a modified position at a slow pace effectively engage the brachialis for arm width.

Starting position is with both arms in a slight angle towards each other at chest height with a dumbbell in each hand. Lower one dumbbell close to full extended arm position slowly and in a controlled manner during the first half of the curl, then curl it back up to starting position, while the other dumbbell stays at its starting position. Now lower the other dumbbell down. This keeps tension on the brachialis. As we pass the 90deg mark on our way down, the biceps will take over the work.

translations.name: Overhead Cable Tricep Extension
translations.description: Use rope handle with your back against the cable machine. Straighten your arms until they are filly extended and reverse the motion resisting on the negative.

translations.name: Lateral Walk
translations.description: Lateral walks, also known as side steps or squat walks, are a type of exercise where you move sideways in a squatting position. They can be performed with or without resistance bands. These exercises strengthen the hip abductors, glutes, and other stabilizing muscles.

translations.name: Leg Press Toe Press
translations.description: Move the leg press using your calves by placing your feet at the bottom of the platform.

translations.name: Handstand
translations.description: Handstand free standing by pressing arms into the ground and contracting core and glutes

translations.name: Cable Fly Lower Chest
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Start position:

    • Arms extended out to your sides in a “Y” shape.

    • Feel a stretch in your chest (slight bend in elbows — don’t lock them).

  2. Movement:

    • Bring your hands downward and together in front of your lower chest or upper abs, in a smooth arc motion.

    • Squeeze your chest hard at the bottom for 1–2 seconds.

  3. Return:

    • Slowly raise your arms back up along the same path until you feel a good stretch in your chest.

    • Maintain control — don’t let the weights pull you back too quickly.

translations.name: Incline Smith Press
translations.description:
• Execution
  • Start position:

  • Unrack the bar and hold it above your upper chest with arms fully extended.

  • Keep your shoulder blades retracted and pressed into the bench.

  • Lowering phase (eccentric):

  • Slowly bring the bar down to just below your collarbone or upper chest.

  • Maintain control — don’t bounce the bar.

  • Pressing phase (concentric):

  • Push the bar upward in a straight line until your arms are fully extended.

  • Focus on squeezing your upper chest at the top.

  • Lock and repeat:

  • Complete your reps, then safely rack the bar back into the hooks.

translations.name: Overhead Triceps Extension
translations.description:
Overhead Triceps Extension with EZ Bar – Quick Guide

The overhead position emphasizes the long head of the triceps (because it's stretched under load), the exercise does engage all three triceps heads (long, lateral, and medial) to some degree during extension.

  1. Setup: Sit on a bench (or stand) and hold an EZ bar with a narrow, overhand grip. Lift the bar overhead, arms fully extended. Keep your core tight and elbows close to your head.

  2. Execution: Slowly lower the bar behind your head by bending your elbows until they reach ~90°. Keep elbows pointed forward (not flaring out).

  3. Finish: Extend your arms back up to the starting position, squeezing your triceps at the top.

Tips:

  • Control the movement to avoid straining your shoulders.

  • Use a moderate weight for full range of motion.

  • Keep your back straight and avoid arching.

translations.name: Incline Static Hold
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Hold Position:

    • Lower the dumbbells a few inches above your chest — about the midpoint of a normal incline press.

    • This is where your upper chest is under maximum tension.

  2. Static Hold:

    • Hold the position for 20–45 seconds while keeping the chest tight.

    • Breathe slowly but stay tense — do not relax your chest or shoulders.

  3. End the set:

    • After the hold, carefully bring the dumbbells down and rest.

translations.name: Pendular hack
translations.description: Pendulum hack, made on a pendulum machine

translations.name: Pendular hack
translations.description: Pendular hack, realizado en máquina pendular

translations.name: Flat Machine Press
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Start position:

    • Elbows bent at roughly 90°, hands just outside your chest.

    • Keep wrists straight and aligned with your forearms.

  2. Pressing phase (concentric):

    • Push the handles forward until your arms are almost fully extended — don’t lock out.

    • Focus on squeezing your chest at the end of the movement.

  3. Returning phase (eccentric):

    • Slowly bring the handles back toward your chest, feeling the stretch in your pecs.

    • Maintain tension — don’t let the weight stack touch down between reps.

translations.name: Sled Push
translations.description:
1. Load the sled with 25% of your maximum load. If you don’t know this, choose a weight you can push for 10 minutes with short breaks. Beginners may choose to push the sled with no weight.
  2. Stand behind the sled and grab the poles with a high-grip hand position.
  3. Engage your core muscles and start pushing the sled forward as fast as you can, powering through your entire leg. Extend your hips and knees as you move the sled forward. Your foot stance should resemble your natural running position.

translations.name: Battle Ropes
translations.description:
1. Hold the ends of the rope at arm's length in front of your hips with your hands shoulder-width apart. 
  2. Brace your core and begin alternately raising and lowering each arm explosively. 
  3. Keep alternating arms for three to four sets of 1 to 2 minutes.

translations.name: Ball Slams
translations.description:
1. Stand with your feet about shoulder-width apart, your knees and hips slightly bent, holding the ball in both hands at chest height. Engage your core, and keep a good posture.
  2. Extend your knees and drive your hips forward while simultaneously lifting the ball. Aim for being as tall as possible, the ball overhead, arms up, hips slightly forward, and on your toes from the force of your drive.
  3. Use your core and arms to slam the medicine ball straight down between your feet with as much force as possible. Press your hips back and bend your knees to further power the slam. Exhale as you slam the ball down.
  4. Squat down to pick up the ball from the floor, then immediately move into the next slam by repeating the movement. Repeat for reps or time.

translations.name: Ski Machine
translations.description:
1. Start standing on the platform with your feet hip-width apart.
  2. Reach overhead to grip the handles with your palms facing in.
  3. Soften your knees, then simultaneously drive your butt back as if you're closing a door behind you while pulling your arms straight down past your hips until your hands pass by the side of your knees.
  4. Next, bring your arms back overhead while thrusting your hips forward until you're standing with your arms fully extended.
  5. Repeat for reps, time or distance.

translations.name: Pendelkniebeube
translations.description: Platziere die Füße in der Mitte der Platte und ca. schulterbreit. Spanne den Bauch an. Entspanne den Nacken während der gesamten Übung.

translations.name: Pendulum Squat
translations.description: Place your feet in the middle of the plate at about shoulder width. Tense your torso. Keep your neck relaxed.

translations.name: Glute Drive
translations.description: Lie down on the back pad and strap yourself in with the waistband. Position yout feet shoulder-length apart. They should be slightly splayed.

translations.name: Toes to bar
translations.description: Der Name „Toes to Bar“ sagt es bereits: Bei dieser Übung, bei der Sie an einer Klimmzugstange hängen, geht es darum, die Zehenspitzen zur Stange Richtung Gesicht zu bringen. Dabei werden die Bauchmuskeln ordentlich gefordert. „Toes to Bar“ sind beliebt im Functional Fitness wie Freeletics oder CrossFit sowie eine wirksame Übung im Sixpack-Training.

translations.name: Toes to bar
translations.description: The name "Toes to Bar" says it all: This exercise, in which you hang from a pull-up bar, involves bringing your toes toward the bar, pointing toward your face. This really works your abdominal muscles. "Toes to Bar" is popular in functional fitness programs like Freeletics and CrossFit, and is an effective exercise for six-pack training.

translations.name: Lying Dumbbell Curls
translations.description: Take a dumbbell in each hand and lie backwards on a bench, similar to a classic chest press. Let your arms hang down at your sides, with the dumbbells possibly touching the floor. Now start to lift the dumbbells upwards until your forearms are perpendicular to the ceiling. Slowly lower the dumbbells until they almost touch the floor again. Keep your ellbows in place to minimize cheating.

translations.name: Cable Curls
translations.description: Bicep Curls using cables. Can be seated or standing

translations.name: Pull-Ups (Wide Grip)
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Pulling phase (concentric):

    • Drive your elbows down and slightly back, pulling your chest toward the bar.

    • Focus on leading with your chest, not your chin.

    • Keep your shoulders depressed (avoid shrugging).

  2. Top position:

    • Chin should clear the bar (or at least reach bar level).

    • Pause for a brief squeeze in your lats.

translations.name: Barbell Row (Overhand)
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Starting position:

    • Hold the barbell with arms fully extended toward the floor.

    • Maintain tension in your lats and back muscles.

translations.name: Barbell Row (Underhand)
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Starting position:

    • Hold the barbell with arms fully extended toward the floor.

    • Maintain tension in your lats and back muscles.

translations.name: lying bicep curl
translations.description: RP fitness bicept curle variant to get a good streach on bicept

translations.name: Delt Stretch
translations.description: Stretch your deltoids for a certain period of time.

translations.name: Pull-up Isometric Hold
translations.description: Hold the pull-up movement in any position

translations.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Hinge at the hips:

    • Push your hips backward, keeping a slight bend in the knees (soft knees).

    • Lower the barbell along the front of your thighs/shins while maintaining a neutral spine.

    • Keep the bar close to your body.

translations.name: One-Arm Heavy Row
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Rowing phase (concentric):

    • Pull the dumbbell toward your lower chest or waist, leading with your elbow.

    • Keep your torso stable — avoid twisting or rotating your shoulders.

    • Pause at the top and squeeze your lats.

translations.name: Jalón al pecho con agarre ancho
translations.description: The lat pulldown is a pulling exercise that primarily targets the latissimus dorsi muscles (commonly known as “lats”) in your back. It involves pulling a cable bar or handle down towards your chest while seated on a machine specifically designed for this exercise. The lat pulldown is typically performed with a wide grip, but can also be done with a narrow grip or underhand grip to target different muscle groups in the back and arms. It is a popular exercise for building upper body strength and improving posture.

translations.name: Jalón al pecho con agarre ancho
translations.description:
Posición inicial
Sentado, los muslos bajo las partes acolchadas, la barra agarrada en pronación, las manos más separadas que el ancho de los hombros. Mantén los codos apuntados hacia afuera y la espalda recta.

Ejecución
Tira de la barra hasta llegar a la barbilla. Mantén la contracción por un momento antes de volver lentamente a la posición inicial. Durante el movimiento, no dejes que tus codos se adelanten.

translations.name: Patadas traseras
translations.description:
The dumbbell kickback is a popular strength training exercise that targets the triceps muscles in the back of your upper arms. It helps strengthen and tone the triceps, contributing to overall arm strength and aesthetics.

Among the exercises that work the arm muscles, kickback and its variations are very effective. You can easily apply these exercises with dumbbells, cables or resistance bands.

translations.name: Patadas traseras
translations.description:
Posición inicial
Coloca tu rodilla izquierda y tu mano izquierda en un banco, con el pecho paralelo al suelo. Mantén el pie derecho en el suelo y agarra la mancuerna con la mano derecha. Manteniéndola cerca de tu cuerpo, levanta la parte superior de tu brazo derecho hasta que esté paralelo al suelo.

Ejecución
Realiza una extensión del brazo derecho. Cuando esté completamente extendido, contrae tu tríceps por un momento antes de volver a la posición inicial. Una vez que hayas completado tu serie, haz lo mismo con el otro brazo.

translations.name: Wide Pull Up
translations.description:
The grip width for a wide grip pull-up is typically wider than 
shoulder-width apart, which means your hands will be positioned farther 
apart than they would be for a standard pull-up. This wider grip targets
 the latissimus dorsi muscles more effectively, making it an excellent 
exercise for building upper body strength, particularly in the back 
muscles.

translations.name: Tuck L-sit
translations.description: The tuck L-sit is a bodyweight hold where you support yourself on parallel bars, parallettes, or the floor with straight arms while pulling your knees toward your chest. Your hips stay lifted, your spine stays tall, and your feet hover off the ground. The goal is to keep your core tight, shoulders depressed, and arms locked out while maintaining the tucked position. It’s a core-intensive, shoulder-stabilizing static hold often used as a progression toward the full L-sit.

translations.name: Archer Pull Up
translations.description: An archer pull-up is an advanced bodyweight exercise that strengthens your back and biceps, serving as a progression towards a one-arm pull-up. It gets its name from the "bow-drawing" position your body takes at the top.Here's how to do it:1. Grip: Grasp a pull-up bar with a wider-than-shoulder-width overhand grip.2. Starting Position: Hang with arms fully extended, engaging your core and keeping your shoulder blades pulled down.3. The Pull: Pull your body up towards one hand, similar to a regular pull-up. At the same time, extend the other arm out to the side, keeping it as straight as possible. Your chin should come towards the hand that is pulling.4. Hold & Lower: Briefly hold the top position where one arm is bent and pulling, and the other is extended. Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position with control.5. Alternate: Repeat the movement, pulling up towards the opposite hand.The key to this exercise is to keep the assisting arm as straight as possible to maximise the load on the working arm. This makes it a challenging but effective exercise for unilateral pulling strength.

translations.name: Larsen Press
translations.description: Put your legs up on a separate Bench and press with no leg drive

translations.name: Bulgarian Squat with Dumbbells
translations.description: The Bulgarian split squat consists of performing a squat on one leg with the rear foot resting on a raised platform. Elevating your back leg on a bench creates instability and increases the range of motion of the exercise.

translations.name: Squats bulgares haltères
translations.description: Le squat bulgare consiste à faire un squat sur une jambe avec le pied arrière en appui sur une plateforme surélevée. Le fait de surélever votre jambe sur un banc crée une instabilité et augmente l'amplitude de mouvement de l'exercice.

translations.name: Stair Master
translations.description: cardio and lower-body strength machine designed to simulate climbing stairs. It provides a low-impact, high-intensity workout that targets the legs, glutes, and core while improving cardiovascular endurance.

translations.name: Stair Master
translations.description: cardio and lower-body strength machine designed to simulate climbing stairs. It provides a low-impact, high-intensity workout that targets the legs, glutes, and core while improving cardiovascular endurance.

translations.name: Elevacion lateral polea
translations.description: elevaciones laterales realizadas unilateralmente en polea

translations.name: Elevación lateral polea
translations.description: Lateral elevation unilateral using a polea

translations.name: Butchers Block Stretch
translations.description:
1. Kneel down with the hands together
  2. Rest the elbows on a bench in front of you and lower the chest down
  3. Keep the elbows bent with good posture and hold

translations.name: Clap Push-UP
translations.description:
The clap push-up is an explosive upper body movement that builds power and fast-twitch muscle strength. It adds a plyometric challenge to the traditional push-up by requiring the hands to leave the ground mid-rep.

  1. Start in a strong push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and core engaged
  2. Lower your body explosively and push off the ground with enough force to lift your hands.
  3. Quickly clap your hands together at chest level before returning them to the floor.
  4. Land with soft elbows to absorb the impact and immediately move into the next rep

translations.name: Sleeper Stretch
translations.description:
1. Lie on your side so that you are resting a little weight on your shoulder blade to keep it still
  2. Extend your arm to 90 degrees in front of you, resting on the floor/mat
  3. Lift your hand with your elbow so that your fingers point to the ceiling and your palm points towards your feet
  4. Keeping your elbow still, gently lower your palm toward the floor

translations.name: Devil’s Press
translations.description:
The Devil’s Press is a hybrid movement combining a dumbbell burpee and a double dumbbell snatch. It’s a full-body, high-intensity exercise that develops strength, power, and metabolic conditioning. 

  1. Start with a dumbbell in each hand and perform a burpee, letting your chest touch the ground while holding the dumbbells.
  2. Explosively jump your feet forward and swing the dumbbells between your legs.
  3. Drive the dumbbells overhead in one continuous motion, locking out your arms.
  4. Lower the dumbbells with control to return to the starting position and repeat

translations.name: Bird Dog
translations.description:
The Bird Dog is a core stability exercise that enhances balance, coordination, and spinal alignment. It’s a low-impact movement ideal for strengthening the posterior chain and improving overall functional control.

  1. Begin on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips, keeping your spine neutral. 
  2. Extend your right arm forward and left leg backward simultaneously, keeping hips square. 
  3. Pause briefly at full extension while engaging your core and glutes. 
  4. Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side

translations.name: Ab wheel
translations.description:
1. Starting Position: Kneel on the floor with the ab wheel in front of you.
  2. Grip the Wheel: Hold the handles firmly.
  3. Roll Out: Slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body while keeping your core tight.
  4. Return: Pull the wheel back towards your knees, maintaining tension in your core.

translations.name: Schwimmen
translations.description: • 800m Schwimmen in 20 Minuten

translations.name: Standing Dowel Shoulder press
translations.description: Mobility - prone dowel press or standing dowel shoulder press alternating between front andback

translations.name: Snap Down
translations.description:
Begin in a standing position with your 
arms up over your head and your toes pushing into the ground raising 
your heels up becoming as tall as you can. From this position, perform a
 small hop with both feet slightly coming off of the ground. As you 
land, bend your knees and begin to hinge forward at your hips absorbing 
the landing. Return to the starting position and repeat. 

You should feel the muscles in your lower body working. 

Start with your arms and knees fully 
straightened out. Keep a stable balance as you land and briefly hold 
that end position, don’t go too fast and become off balanced. Keep your 
chest up. 

For a detailed video on landing mechanics, click here: https://youtu.be/RThUCYRDyZw

translations.name: Bretzel stretch
translations.description: How to Perform the Bretzel Stretch    Starting Position:        Lie on your back on a flat surface, such as a mat.        Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the ground.    Leg Positioning:        Lift your right leg and cross it over your left leg, placing your right foot on the outside of your left knee.        Your left leg should remain flat on the ground.    Arm Positioning:        Extend your left arm out to the side at shoulder height, keeping it straight.        Use your right hand to gently pull your right knee towards the floor on the left side of your body.    Stretching:        As you pull your knee down, try to keep your left shoulder flat on the ground.        You should feel a stretch in your hip, lower back, and possibly your chest.    Hold the Position:        Maintain this position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and relaxing into the stretch.        Switch sides and repeat the process.

translations.name: Hip hinge
translations.description: Hip Hinge Exercise DescriptionThe hip hinge is a fundamental movement pattern that involves bending at the hips while keeping the spine neutral. It is commonly used in exercises like deadlifts, kettlebell swings, and good mornings. To perform a hip hinge:    Starting Position: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.    Hinge at the Hips: Push your hips back while maintaining a straight back. Your torso should lean forward, and your chest should stay up.    Lowering Phase: Continue to hinge until your torso is nearly parallel to the ground, or until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.    Return to Standing: Drive through your heels and thrust your hips forward to return to the starting position.

translations.name: Bronco
translations.description:
Finess test for rugby.5 x shuttle runs of 20m, 40m, and 60m (20yd, 45yd, 65yd/far 45yd mark)
Complete as fast as possible

translations.name: Perpendicular Unilateral Landmine Row
translations.description: Using a landmine attachment with the barbell running at 90 degrees to either side, hinge slightly at the hips and grip the barbell by the plate sleeve. Raise the barbell to chest height, with your upper arm parallel to the floor at the top of the movement.

translations.name: Trap press
translations.description: Laying flat with arm pointing straight upwards, elbow fully extended, use only your shoulder to raise the weight as high as comfortable and back to rest.

translations.name: Side-laying interior rotation
translations.description: Laying on your side and using the arm closest to the bench, maintain a 90 degree bend at the elbow and allow the weight to fall towards the ground past the bench as low as comfortable. Bring the weight up using only rotation of the shoulder to the opposing shoulder.

translations.name: Supine press
translations.description: Take a close grip and push perpendicular to the bench. Keep back flat.

translations.name: March or jog in place
translations.description: Low-impact cardiovascular exercise that simulates running without moving, ideal for warming up, improving endurance, or training in small spaces.

translations.name: Marcha o trote en el lugar
translations.description: Ejercicio cardiovascular de bajo impacto que simula correr sin desplazamiento, ideal para calentar, mejorar la resistencia o entrenar en espacios reducidos.

translations.name: Estiramiento de piernas y cadera
translations.description: Movimientos suaves para mejorar la flexibilidad de la parte inferior del cuerpo y liberar tensión en la cadera.

translations.name: Estiramiento de brazos y cuello
translations.description: Alivia tensiones en la parte superior del cuerpo, especialmente útil para personas con trabajo de oficina o posturas prolongadas.

translations.name: Respiración profunda (de pie o sentado)
translations.description: Técnica de respiración consciente para mejorar oxigenación, reducir el estrés y conectar con el momento presente.

translations.name: Meditación guiada o libre
translations.description: Ejercicio mental de atención plena (mindfulness) que ayuda a reducir ansiedad, mejorar el enfoque y el bienestar general.

translations.name: Smith Machine Split Squat
translations.description: The "Smith Machine Split Squat" is a strength exercise performed on the Smith machine, ideal for targeting the legs and glutes. It involves placing one foot forward and the other back, lowering the body until the front thigh is parallel to the ground while the bar slides vertically along the machine. This exercise helps improve stability, correct muscle imbalances, and reduces stress on the back, providing a safe environment for strength training.

translations.name: Smith Machine Split Squat
translations.description: The "Smith Machine Split Squat" is a strength exercise performed on the Smith machine, ideal for targeting the legs and glutes. It involves placing one foot forward and the other back, lowering the body until the front thigh is parallel to the ground while the bar slides vertically along the machine. This exercise helps improve stability, correct muscle imbalances, and reduces stress on the back, providing a safe environment for strength training.

translations.name: Pull-Ups (Neutral Grip)
translations.description:
Execution

  1. Pulling phase (concentric):

    • Drive your elbows down and back, pulling your chest toward your hands.

    • Keep your shoulders depressed and retracted (avoid shrugging).

    • Focus on squeezing your lats and upper back at the top.

  2. Top position:

    • Chin should clear or reach the level of your hands.

    • Pause briefly and contract your lats hard.

translations.name: Reverse Fly Standing
translations.description:
1. Stand with a good posture holding a cable in each hand opposite the machine
  2. Starting with the hands in front of your chest pull the arms out to your side into the shape of a T with the elbows nearly straight
  3. Slowly release the arms forward to the start
  4. Repeat with the arms moving into the shape of a Y
  5. Repeat with the arms in the shape of a W
  6. Repeat with the arms in the shape of an L by your side

notes.comment: The full described cycle is one rep.

translations.name: Horizontal Shoulder Flexion Stretch
translations.description:
1. Raise your arm out to the side, placing your hand on a wall or doorway beside you at shoulder height
  2. Turn your body away from your arm to stretch the chest, as far as is comfortable

notes.comment: Gently push your hand forward against the wall/doorway, keeping your elbow straight

translations.name: Jalon caballero unialteral
translations.description: Pull performed in knight's stance

translations.name: Jalón caballero unialteral
translations.description: Jalón unilateral realizado en postura de caballero. La rodilla que se apoya en el suelo es la misma de la mano que hace la fuerza

translations.name: Dumbbell Scaption
translations.description:
Starting position:

Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.

Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).

Keep a slight bend in your elbows, shoulders relaxed and shoulder blades pulled back.

Execution:

Slowly raise the dumbbells forward and slightly to the side, at about a 30–45° angle from your body (between a front raise and a lateral raise).

Lift until your arms are about shoulder height (do not go overhead).

Pause for a second at the top and feel the activation in your shoulders and scapular stabilizers.

Lower the dumbbells back down under control to the starting position.

Breathing:

Inhale as you lower the dumbbells.

Exhale as you lift them.

Common mistakes:

Using dumbbells that are too heavy (causing jerky movements).

Lifting above shoulder height (puts unnecessary stress on the shoulder joint).

Arching the lower back instead of keeping the movement controlled at the shoulders.

Purpose of the exercise:

Activates the lower trapezius, serratus anterior, and scapular stabilizers.

Helps correct posture and improve shoulder balance.

translations.name: Dumbbell Scaption
translations.description:
Početni položaj:

Stani uspravno, stopala u širini kukova.

U svakoj ruci drži po bučicu neutralnim hvatom (dlanovi okrenuti jedan prema drugome).

Ruke lagano savijene u laktovima, ramena spuštena i zategnuta lopatice.

Izvođenje pokreta:

Polako podiži bučice prema naprijed i u stranu, pod kutom od oko 30–45° od tijela (između prednjeg i bočnog podizanja).

Podiži sve dok ruke ne dođu u visinu ramena (ne iznad glave).

Zadrži sekundu na vrhu i osjeti aktivaciju mišića ramena i lopatica.

Kontrolirano spuštaj bučice nazad u početni položaj.

Disanje:

Udah dok spuštaš bučice.

Izdisaj dok podižeš.

Najčešće greške:

Dizanje preteških bučica (loša kontrola, trzanje).

Podizanje iznad visine ramena (povećava rizik od prenaprezanja).

„Slomljena“ forma – podizanje iz donjeg dijela leđa umjesto iz ramena.

Cilj vježbe:

Aktivira donji trapez, serratus anterior i stabilizatore lopatice.

Pomaže u ispravljanju držanja i balansiranju ramena.

translations.name: Leg curl con elastico
translations.description: In posizione eretta: posiziona l'elastico sotto i piedi a centro piede e afferralo con presa a martello.

translations.name: Leg curl with elastic
translations.description: Standing position: Place the band under your feet in the middle of your foot and grasp it with a hammer grip.

translations.name: Affondo laterale con pattina
translations.description: n piedi, piedi alla larghezza delle anche, tieni un kettlebell vicino al petto in posizione goblet. Posiziona un piede su una pattina/disco scorrevole. Mantieni il busto eretto e il core attivo. Fai scivolare lentamente il piede verso l’esterno piegando il ginocchio della gamba opposta, abbassando i fianchi in un affondo laterale. Mantieni il ginocchio della gamba di lavoro allineato con le dita del piede, evitando che collassi verso l’interno. La gamba non in appoggio resta distesa e scivola fluidamente sul pavimento. Spingi con il tallone della gamba piegata per tornare alla posizione iniziale, mantenendo il controllo del kettlebell.

translations.name: Sliding Lateral Lunge
translations.description: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart, holding a kettlebell close to your chest in goblet position. Place one foot on a slider, gliding disc, or towel. Keep your chest tall and core engaged. Slowly slide the foot outward to the side while bending the opposite knee, lowering your hips into a lateral lunge. Keep the working leg’s knee aligned with the toes and avoid letting it collapse inward. The non-working leg remains straight and slides smoothly along the floor. Push through the heel of the bent leg to return to the starting position while maintaining control of the kettlebell.

translations.name: Copenhagen Adduction Exercise
translations.description: Lie on your side with the elbow placed directly under the shoulder, similar to a side plank position. Place the upper leg on a bench with the inside of the foot or ankle resting on it. Lift your hips and hold your body in a straight line while keeping the lower leg off the floor. The exercise strongly activates the adductors of the upper leg while also challenging the core and hip stabilizers.

translations.name: Copenhagen Plank
translations.description: Sdraiati su un fianco con il gomito appoggiato sotto la spalla, in posizione simile a una plank laterale. Appoggia la gamba superiore su una panca con il lato interno del piede o della caviglia. Solleva i fianchi e mantieni il corpo in linea retta tenendo la gamba inferiore sollevata da terra. L’esercizio attiva intensamente gli adduttori della gamba in appoggio e coinvolge anche il core e i muscoli stabilizzatori dell’anca.

translations.name: Arm Raises (T/Y/I)
translations.description:
Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, core engaged, and arms relaxed at your sides. From this position, perform three different arm raises to target the shoulders and upper back:

  • T Raise: Lift your arms straight out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, forming a “T” shape.

  • Y Raise: Lift your arms upward at about a 45° angle from your body to form a “Y” shape.

  • I Raise: Raise your arms straight overhead, close together, forming an “I” shape.

Maintain a neutral spine, avoid arching your lower back, and move slowly with control. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping your shoulders down away from your ears.

translations.name: Typewriter Pull-ups
translations.description: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width. Pull yourself up until your chin is above the bar. At the top position, instead of going straight down, shift your body to one side by extending one arm while keeping the other arm bent. Move smoothly from one side to the other, like a typewriter motion, before lowering yourself back down. This exercise increases time under tension and strengthens the lats, biceps, and shoulders while building unilateral pulling strength.

translations.name: Підтягування-друкарська машинка
translations.description: Підтягуєшся вгору, утримуєш верхню точку → переводиш тіло вліво → вправо → опускаєшся.

translations.name: Bodyweight Biceps Curl
translations.description: Set up under a straight bar (around waist or chest height). Grip the bar with your palms facing you (supinated grip), hands about shoulder-width apart. Lean back with straight arms so that your body is at an angle to the ground. Keeping your elbows high and close to the bar, pull your upper body toward the bar by flexing your elbows, similar to a biceps curl. Lower yourself back down in a controlled manner. Keep your core tight and movement slow to maximize tension on the biceps.

translations.name: Glute Kickback (Machine)
translations.description:
Drive through your heel, not your toes, to hit the glute.  
 Don’t hyperextend your lower back at the top.

translations.name: Standing Adduction (Cable)
translations.description:
Keep your hips square and still, pulling the cable across your opposing leg using only your inner thigh.
Don’t let your torso lean or twist — no side bending to help the rep.

translations.name: Seated Row (Machine)
translations.description:
Keep your chest up and squeeze your shoulder blades at the end.  
 Don’t hunch forward or round your back during the pull.

translations.name: Sentadilla con pesa rusa
kettlebell sumo deadlift
translations.description:
Place your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, pointing your toes outward.
Keep your back straight, shoulders back, and chest up.
The knees must follow the same direction as the toes and not move forward.
When going up, it is advisable to contract your glutes to maximize the effectiveness of the exercise.

images.license_title: Patada de glúteo con banda elastica

translations.name: Patada de glúteo con banda elástica
translations.description: Una patada de glúteos con banda elástica es un ejercicio que se realiza a gatas. Se ancla una banda de resistencia a la parte posterior de la rodilla y se patea el talón hacia atrás y arriba, apretando el glúteo en la parte superior del movimiento, manteniendo el torso estable y la espalda recta para trabajar y fortalecer los músculos de los glúteos.

translations.name: rubber band glute kickback
translations.description: A rubber band glute kickback is an exercise performed on hands and knees, where you anchor a resistance band to your foot and kick your heel back and up, squeezing your glute at the top of the movement, while maintaining a stable core and flat back to target and strengthen the gluteal muscles.

translations.name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
translations.description:
Dumbbell hip thrust
Begin seated on the ground with a bench placed horizontally behind you and your knees bent. Lay a dumbbell across your hips, holding either end with both hands. You may wish to place a pad or towel on your hips to increase comfort. With knees bent and feet firmly planted on the floor, lean back onto the bench so that it is gently pressing into your upper back. This is your starting position.

Inhale. Exhale. Holding onto the dumbbell, press your heels into the mat and raise your hips off the floor until your body forms one straight line from chin to knee, resting your shoulders on the bench and ensuring that your head is an extension of your spine.

Inhale. Lower your hips to return to the starting position but without resting your glutes on the mat. Repeat for the specified number of repetitions.

translations.name: Elevacion de cadera con mancuernas
translations.description:
Elevación de cadera con mancuernas
Comienza sentado en el suelo con un banco horizontal detrás de ti y las rodillas flexionadas. Coloca una mancuerna sobre tus caderas, sujetando cada extremo con ambas manos. Puedes colocar una almohadilla o toalla sobre las caderas para mayor comodidad. Con las rodillas flexionadas y los pies firmemente apoyados en el suelo, inclínate hacia atrás sobre el banco de modo que presione suavemente la parte superior de la espalda. Esta es la posición inicial.

Inhala. Exhala. Sujetando la mancuerna, presiona los talones contra la colchoneta y levanta las caderas del suelo hasta que tu cuerpo forme una línea recta desde la barbilla hasta la rodilla, apoyando los hombros en el banco y asegurándote de que la cabeza sea una extensión de la columna vertebral.

Inhala. Baja las caderas para volver a la posición inicial, pero sin apoyar los glúteos en la colchoneta. Repite el número de repeticiones indicado.

translations.name: Cardio en cinta
Treadmill Cardio
translations.description:
It's a cardiovascular workout performed on a treadmill to improve endurance, burn calories, and promote heart health. It offers multiple benefits, such as the ability to easily adjust speed and incline, allowing for a variety of workouts, from hill walking to high-intensity interval training (HIIT) and tempo runs. To optimize results, it's important to vary your workouts.

Benefits of Treadmill Cardio:
- Improved Cardiovascular Health:
Regular aerobic exercise on a treadmill strengthens the heart and lungs, which can help prevent heart disease.
- Weight Loss:
By burning calories, treadmill training can be an effective tool for weight loss and maintaining a healthy weight.
- Muscle Strengthening:
It strengthens the muscles in the legs and glutes, helping to prevent muscle loss, which is especially important with age.
- Training Flexibility:
Allows you to vary speed, incline, and intervals, making your workout more dynamic and adaptable to different fitness levels.

Types of Treadmill Training:
- Hill Walking:
Increasing the incline of the treadmill and walking at a brisk pace can improve endurance and work your leg muscles.
- Endurance Jog:
A run at a comfortable, steady pace that keeps your heart rate in a target zone (such as Zone 2) to build endurance.
- Interval Training:
Alternating periods of high intensity (fast running) with periods of low intensity (easy walking or jogging) to maximize calorie burn and cardiovascular improvement.
- Tempo Run:
A run at a steady, sustained pace that is slightly faster than a normal jog, which challenges your ability to sustain a more intense effort for an extended period.

translations.name: Patada de burro con mancuerna
translations.description:
La patada de burro con mancuerna es un ejercicio efectivo para trabajar los músculos de los glúteos y los isquiotibiales. Este ejercicio se realiza generalmente en una posición de cuatro patas, utilizando una mancuerna para aumentar la resistencia. A continuación, se describen los pasos para realizarlo correctamente.
Posición Inicial: Colócate en una posición de cuatro patas sobre una colchoneta, con las manos alineadas con los hombros y las rodillas alineadas con las caderas.
Colocación de la Mancuerna: Toma una mancuerna y colócala detrás de la rodilla de la pierna que vas a levantar. Asegúrate de que esté bien sujeta para evitar que se caiga durante el ejercicio.
Ejercicio:
Inhala y, al exhalar, levanta la pierna con la mancuerna hacia arriba, manteniendo la rodilla flexionada en un ángulo de 90 grados.
Eleva la pierna hasta que esté paralela al suelo, sintiendo la contracción en los glúteos.
Mantén la posición durante un segundo en la parte superior y luego baja lentamente la pierna a la posición inicial.
Repeticiones: Realiza de 10 a 15 repeticiones por cada pierna, asegurándote de mantener una buena forma durante todo el ejercicio.
Series: Completa de 2 a 3 series, dependiendo de tu nivel de condición física.

translations.name: Crunch en maquina
translations.description:
El crunch en máquina es un ejercicio diseñado para trabajar los músculos abdominales, específicamente el recto abdominal. Este ejercicio se realiza en una máquina específica que proporciona soporte y resistencia, lo que puede ayudar a mejorar la forma y la efectividad del movimiento.

Beneficios del Crunch en Máquina
Aislamiento Muscular: Permite un enfoque más directo en los músculos abdominales.
Soporte: La máquina proporciona soporte para la espalda, lo que puede ser beneficioso para quienes tienen problemas de espalda.
Control de Resistencia: Puedes ajustar la resistencia según tu nivel de fuerza y experiencia.
Cómo Realizar el Crunch en Máquina
Ajusta la Máquina: Asegúrate de que la máquina esté configurada a tu altura y que el asiento esté en la posición correcta.
Siéntate: Coloca tus pies en los reposapiés y agarra las manijas.
Posición Inicial: Inclina ligeramente la parte superior del cuerpo hacia atrás, manteniendo la espalda recta.
Ejercicio: Contrae los abdominales y lleva el torso hacia adelante, asegurándote de no usar el impulso.
Regresa: Vuelve lentamente a la posición inicial.

translations.name: Cardio en bicicleta estática
translations.description:
Beneficios del Cardio en Bicicleta Estática
El cardio en bicicleta estática es una excelente forma de ejercicio que ofrece múltiples beneficios para la salud y el bienestar. Aquí te detallo algunos de los más destacados:

Mejora de la Salud Cardiovascular
Fortalece el corazón: El ejercicio regular ayuda a mejorar la eficiencia del corazón y a reducir el riesgo de enfermedades cardiovasculares.
Aumenta la circulación: Mejora el flujo sanguíneo, lo que puede ayudar a reducir la presión arterial.
Quema de Calorías y Pérdida de Peso
Eficiente para quemar calorías: Dependiendo de la intensidad, puedes quemar entre 400 y 600 calorías por hora.
Ayuda en la pérdida de peso: Combinado con una dieta equilibrada, el cardio en bicicleta estática puede ser efectivo para perder peso.
Fortalecimiento Muscular
Tonifica las piernas: Trabaja principalmente los músculos de las piernas, incluyendo cuádriceps, isquiotibiales y pantorrillas.
Mejora la resistencia muscular: Con el tiempo, puedes aumentar la resistencia y la fuerza en las piernas.
Beneficios para la Salud Mental
Reduce el estrés: El ejercicio libera endorfinas, que pueden mejorar tu estado de ánimo y reducir el estrés.
Aumenta la energía: La actividad física regular puede aumentar tus niveles de energía y mejorar la calidad del sueño.

translations.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown (Cable)
translations.description:
Keep arms straight but not locked, pulling with your lats, not your shoulders.  
 Don’t let your shoulders shrug up during the movement.

translations.name: Seated Dumbbell Calf Raise
translations.description: The seated dumbbell calf raise is a targeted exercise for strengthening the lower leg muscles. It follows the same movement pattern as the machine version: you place the weight on your knees, and by extending and flexing your ankles, you move the weight.

translations.name: Élévation des mollets haltères assis
translations.description: L'élévation des mollets avec haltères en position assise est un exercice ciblé pour renforcer les muscles du bas de la jambe. Il suit le même schéma de mouvement que la version avec machine : vous placez le poids sur vos genoux, et en étendant et en fléchissant vos chevilles, vous déplacez le poids.

translations.name: Unilateral Cable row
translations.description: Put a vertical seated bench in front of the cable row machine.position it so that you can focus on one side, and then do slow pulls focusing on the lateral muscles and ensuring tention in those muscles when in a stretched position.

translations.name: Side Straight-Arm Pulldown (Cable)
translations.description:
Keep your torso still, pulling the handle down along your side using the outer lat.  
 Don’t twist your body or rotate your torso to cheat the rep.

translations.name: Shoulder Internal Rotation (Cable)
translations.description:
Keep your elbow tucked to your side like it's superglued there.  
 Don’t rotate your torso — only your forearm should move.

translations.name: Shoulder External Rotation (Cable)
translations.description:
Keep elbow fixed at your side and rotate forearm outward smoothly.  
 Don’t let your wrist or elbow drift upward — stay in one plane.

translations.name: Side Lateral Raise (Cable)
translations.description:
Lift with your elbows leading, not your hands.  
 Don’t shrug your shoulders; keep traps quiet.

translations.name: Front Raise (Cable)
