translations.description:
Raise the handle in a slight arc with controlled movement.  
 Don’t swing your torso to start the rep.

translations.name: Face pulls with yellow/green band
translations.description: Face pulls with band – standing horizontal pull. Grab the band at face height, pull elbows high and wide towards your face while squeezing shoulder blades together. Trains upper back, rear shoulders and improves posture.

translations.name: Isometric Squat to Failure
translations.description:
sometric Squat to Failure: A Strength Training Technique
Definition

The isometric squat to failure is a variation of the squat exercise where you hold a static squat position at a specific angle, maintaining the position until your muscles can no longer sustain the contraction.

translations.name: Sentadilla isometrica al fallo
translations.description:
Sentadilla Isométrica al Fallo

Definición
La sentadilla isométrica al fallo es una variante de ejercicio de sentadilla donde mantienes una posición estática de sentadilla en un ángulo específico, sosteniendo la posición hasta que los músculos no pueden mantener más la contracción.

Tipo de Contracción Isométrica (estática, sin movimiento)
Grupos Musculares Principales Cuádriceps, glúteos, músculos posteriores del muslo
Intensidad Muy alta, trabaja hasta el agotamiento muscular
Beneficios Mejora fuerza, resistencia muscular, estabilidad
Técnica de Ejecución
Colócate en posición de sentadilla con los pies separados al ancho de las caderas
Desciende hasta formar un ángulo de 90 grados en las rodillas
Mantén esa posición completamente inmóvil
Resiste hasta que los músculos fallen y no puedas sostener más la posición

Beneficios
    Desarrollo de fuerza máxima
    Mejora de la resistencia muscular
    Fortalecimiento de la estabilidad articular
    Incremento de la tolerancia al dolor muscular

Precauciones
    No recomendado para principiantes
    Riesgo de fatiga muscular intensa
    Consultar con un profesional antes de implementarlo
    Usar con moderación en la rutina de entrenamiento

images.license_title: Zancada con pesa rusa a una pierna

translations.name: Single-Leg Lunge with Kettlebell:
translations.description:
How to do a single-leg kettlebell lunge

Preparation: Stand with your feet together. Hold the kettlebell with both hands by the handle, keeping it close to your chest, or hold it with one hand (on the same side as the working leg).
Step and Lower: Take a large step forward (or backward, which is safer for your knees) with one leg, lowering your hips until both knees form a 90-degree angle.
Focus: Shift your weight onto your front (or bent) leg, keeping your back straight and your torso upright.
Return: Push off with your front leg to return to the starting position.

translations.name: Zancada con pesa rusa a una pierna
translations.description:
La "zancada con pesa rusa a una pierna" (o unilateral)
es un ejercicio que mejora el equilibrio y la fuerza, implicando sujetar la pesa rusa cerca del pecho o con un brazo (mismo lado de la pierna de apoyo) y bajar en una zancada profunda, alternando piernas, o hacer variantes como sentadillas búlgaras con kettlebell, enfocándose en la estabilidad y el trabajo unilateral del tren inferior para fortalecer cuádriceps, glúteos y femorales, con versiones hacia adelante, atrás o laterales. 
Cómo hacer la zancada unilateral con pesa rusa

Preparación: Ponte de pie con los pies juntos. Sujeta la pesa rusa con ambas manos por el asa, manteniéndola pegada al pecho, o sosténla con una sola mano (del mismo lado que la pierna que trabajará).
Paso y descenso: Da un gran paso hacia adelante (o hacia atrás, que es más seguro para las rodillas) con una pierna, bajando la cadera hasta que ambas rodillas formen un ángulo de 90 grados.
Enfoque: Desplaza el peso hacia la pierna delantera (o la que se flexiona), manteniendo la espalda recta y el torso erguido.
Regreso: Empuja con la pierna delantera para volver a la posición inicial.

images.license_title: Press de gluteo a una pierna

translations.name: Gluteo en prensa de lado con una pierna
translations.description:
Colócate en la máquina de prensa de piernas, pero en lugar de sentarte de frente, siéntate de lado para que la pierna que vas a trabajar quede alineada con la plataforma móvil. Asegura tu espalda contra el respaldo y agárrate bien de las asas para mantener la estabilidad
Coloca el pie de la pierna de trabajo en la plataforma, asegurándote de que el talón quede bien apoyado y que la pierna esté casi completamente extendida, pero sin bloquear la rodilla. Desbloquea la seguridad de la máquina. Baja la plataforma lentamente y con control, doblando la rodilla y la cadera. El objetivo es llevar el talón lo más cerca posible de tu glúteo sin que la zona lumbar se despegue del respaldo. Es crucial mantener la espalda recta y no encorvarse.
Empuja la plataforma hacia arriba usando principalmente la fuerza del glúteo y el cuádriceps, volviendo a la posición inicial (pierna casi extendida). Concéntrate en la contracción muscular en la parte superior del movimiento 
Evita rebotar o usar el impulso
Comienza con un peso ligero para dominar la técnica y evitar lesiones, especialmente en la espalda baja o las rodillas.

translations.name: Single-leg side glute press
translations.description:
The Single-Leg Side Glute Press is a leg press machine variation where you angle your body sideways on the pad, placing one foot high and slightly angled to press the weight, powerfully targeting the outer glute (gluteus medius/minimus) and hamstrings for balanced leg development and stability, requiring focus on pushing through the heel and maintaining control without locking out. 
How to Perform:

    Setup: Sit in the leg press machine, rotate your body to one hip, placing one foot high on the platform at about a 45-degree angle with toes pointed slightly out/forward.
    Positioning: Keep your hip, knee, and ankle aligned, and ensure your lower back stays on the pad.
    Descent: Slowly lower the weight, allowing your knee to bend deeply (near 90 degrees), feeling a stretch in your glute and hamstring.
    Press: Drive through your heel (not your toes) to press the weight back up, squeezing your glutes and hamstrings, stopping just before your leg fully extends.
    Control: Avoid bouncing and don't let your lower back lift off the pad; maintain tension throughout the movement.

images.license_title: Peso muerto a una pierna con mancuerna

translations.name: Peso muerto a una pierna con mancuerna
translations.description:
Postura inicial:
Sostén una mancuerna en la mano opuesta a la pierna que vas a utilizar como soporte. (Si te paras sobre la pierna derecha, sostén la mancuerna con la mano izquierda). Esto ayuda a contrarrestar el peso y activa los músculos estabilizadores.
Mantén la espalda recta y el core activado.
Mueve ligeramente el peso de tu cuerpo a la pierna de apoyo, con una mínima flexión en la rodilla.

El movimiento (la bisagra de cadera):
Inicia el movimiento doblando la cadera hacia atrás, no doblando las rodillas. Imagina que empujas un interruptor con los glúteos.
Simultáneamente, extiende la pierna libre hacia atrás para mantener el equilibrio. Tu cuerpo debería formar una línea recta desde la cabeza hasta el talón de la pierna extendida.
Baja la mancuerna hacia el suelo, manteniendo el brazo extendido. El objetivo es que tu torso quede casi paralelo al suelo, o hasta donde tu flexibilidad te lo permita sin curvar la espalda baja.

El regreso:
Haz una pausa breve en la parte inferior del movimiento.
Empuja el suelo con el talón de la pierna de apoyo y contrae los glúteos para volver a la posición inicial, llevando la pierna trasera de vuelta al lado de la de apoyo.
Mantén el control en todo momento.

translations.name: Single-Leg Deadlift with Dumbbell
translations.description:
Starting Position: Stand upright with a slight bend in your knees, holding a dumbbell in one or both hands. Shift your weight onto your supporting leg (e.g., your right leg) and lift your non-supporting foot slightly off the floor.
The Hinge: Keeping your back flat and core engaged, hinge at your hips, pushing your glutes backward. As your torso lowers, extend your non-supporting leg straight back behind you for balance, keeping your hips square to the floor. The dumbbell(s) should lower toward the ground along the line of your supporting leg.
Range of Motion: Continue lowering your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor, or you feel an intense stretch in your supporting hamstring, ensuring your back remains neutral throughout the movement.
Return to Start: Pause at the bottom, then contract your glutes and hamstrings to slowly raise your torso back to the starting position. Your non-supporting leg should return in line with the supporting one.
Switch Sides: After completing your desired number of repetitions on one side, switch your weight and repeat the movement on the other leg.

translations.name: Assisted chin-ups
translations.description: Chin-ups with machine assistance (counterweights)

translations.name: Neutral-grip pull-ups or TRX rows
translations.description:
• Pull-Up: Vertical Pull — Focuses more on the Lats (back width).

    TRX Row: Horizontal Pull — Focuses more on the Rhomboids/Traps (mid-back thickness and posture).

translations.name: Shrimp Squad
translations.description: Single leg squad where one leg is behind you.

translations.name: Single Leg Glute Bridge
translations.description: A glute bridge, where you use just one leg at a time.

translations.name: L-Sit Pull-ups
translations.description: L-Sit Pull-ups train both upper body and core muscles.

translations.name: Back Lever
translations.description: Hanging from a pull-up bar facing downwards with your arms extended

translations.name: Windshield Wipers
translations.description: Hang from a pull-up bar, raise your legs to the ceiling. Move lower body from left to right with straight legs like a windshield wiper

translations.name: Elevación lateral en maquina
translations.description:
Técnica de Ejecución

La elevación lateral en máquina es un ejercicio diseñado para trabajar específicamente el músculo deltoides lateral (hombro medio) con un alto grado de control y seguridad.

Pasos de Ejecución:
    Ajuste la máquina a su altura y posición correcta
    Siéntese con la espalda apoyada firmemente contra el respaldo
    Agarre los mangos laterales con un agarre cómodo
    Mantenga los codos ligeramente flexionados
    Eleve los brazos lateralmente hasta la altura de los hombros
    Pause brevemente en la posición superior
    Baje los brazos de manera controlada a la posición inicial

Beneficios
    Aislamiento preciso del deltoides lateral
    Menor riesgo de lesiones en comparación con elevaciones libres
    Control del rango de movimiento
    Ideal para principiantes y rehabilitación

Consejos Importantes
    Evite balancear o hacer movimientos de impulso
    Mantenga un movimiento controlado y constante
    No sobrecargue el peso
    Respire de manera apropiada: exhale al levantar, inhale al bajar

translations.name: Machine Side Lateral Raises
translations.description:
Machine side lateral raises are an isolation exercise for the side (lateral) deltoids, performed on a machine for stability, targeting the outer shoulder by lifting pads or handles out to the sides to shoulder height, then lowering slowly, keeping elbows slightly bent and traps down to focus on the delts. This exercise helps build shoulder width by preventing swinging and providing constant tension, making it great for beginners or as a finisher. 
How to Perform Machine Lateral Raises

    Setup: Adjust the seat so your shoulders align with the machine's pivot point, your chest is against the pad (or back if facing out), and your arms are at a 90-degree angle with the pads/handles.
    Starting Position: Grip the handles with a relaxed grip, elbows slightly bent, and shoulders down (depressed) away from your ears.
    Lifting Phase: Exhale and lift your arms out to the sides, moving your upper arms laterally, until they are parallel to the floor.
    Peak Contraction: Pause briefly at the top, squeezing the side delts, but don't shrug your shoulders up.
    Lowering Phase: Inhale and slowly lower the weight back down with control, resisting the weight on the way down.

translations.name: Cable Front Raise with a small bar
translations.description:
The Cable Front Raise with a small bar isolates your anterior deltoids (front shoulders) by providing constant tension, requiring you to stand facing away from a low pulley, grab the bar with an overhand grip, and lift it in front of you to shoulder height with slightly bent arms, avoiding swinging and keeping your core tight for controlled movement up and down. Use a light-to-moderate weight, focusing on slow, controlled reps to feel the muscle work, not momentum. 
How to Perform the Cable Front Raise (Bar)

    Setup: Attach a small straight bar to a low cable pulley. Set the weight to a light or moderate setting.
    Starting Position: Stand with your back to the machine, holding the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) at hip level, arms extended but not locked. Feet shoulder-width apart, core braced, shoulders back and down.
    The Lift: Exhale and slowly raise the bar straight up in front of you, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until your hands reach shoulder height (or slightly below).
    The Hold: Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your front delts.
    The Lower: Inhale as you slowly and controllably lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension.

translations.name: Elevación frontal en polea con barra
translations.description:
Posición inicial: Colócate de espaldas a la polea baja. Agarra la barra recta (o barra Z) con un agarre prono (palmas hacia abajo), a la anchura de los hombros. Da un paso atrás para que la barra quede a la altura de tus muslos, con las rodillas ligeramente flexionadas y el core activado para proteger la zona lumbar.
Fase de elevación: Levanta la barra hacia adelante en un arco, manteniendo los codos semiflexionados. Sube hasta que la barra esté a la altura de los hombros o un poco por debajo.
Contracción: En la parte superior, aprieta el deltoides anterior (parte frontal del hombro) durante un instante.
Fase de descenso: Baja el peso de forma lenta y controlada hasta la posición inicial. La fase excéntrica (bajada) es crucial para el desarrollo muscular. 

Consejos clave
    Evita el balanceo: Mantén el torso firme y no uses el impulso del cuerpo. Si te balanceas, el peso es demasiado alto.
    Codos: Mantenlos ligeramente flexionados y fijos; no los extiendas por completo ni los subas excesivamente.
    Enfoque: Concéntrate en sentir el trabajo en el deltoides anterior. Es un movimiento de flexión de hombro, no de bíceps.

translations.name: Floor dumbbell bench press
translations.description: This exercise involves lying on your back with a dumbbell in each hand, and pressing them up towards the ceiling, targeting the chest muscles. The dumbbells are then lowered back down to the starting position on the floor.

translations.name: Sentadilla multipower
translations.description:
Técnica y ejecución
La sentadilla en máquina Smith es una variante de la sentadilla con barra tradicional que utiliza una barra guiada con una trayectoria fija. Aquí tienes un desglose detallado de la técnica correcta:

Posición inicial
Colocación de los pies: Coloca los pies ligeramente por delante de la barra, separados aproximadamente a la anchura de los hombros.
Posición de la barra: Apoya la barra sobre la parte superior del trapecio/hombros, similar a una sentadilla tradicional.
Postura: Los pies pueden estar ligeramente inclinados hacia afuera para una rotación natural de la cadera.

Movimiento de la sentadilla
Descarga la barra girándola para soltarla de los ganchos de seguridad.
Baja lentamente el cuerpo flexionando las rodillas y las caderas.
Desciende hasta que los muslos estén paralelos al suelo (o ligeramente por debajo).
Haz una breve pausa al final del movimiento.
Impulsa los talones para volver a la posición inicial.

Beneficios y consideraciones
Ventajas
Requiere menos equilibrio: La trayectoria fija de la barra facilita el movimiento para principiantes.
Movimiento controlado: Menor riesgo de mala postura en comparación con las sentadillas con peso libre.
Aislamiento: Permite el desarrollo muscular de las piernas.

translations.name: Smith machine squat
translations.description:
Technique and Execution

The Smith machine squat is a variation of the traditional barbell squat that uses a guided, fixed-path barbell. Here's a detailed breakdown of the proper technique:
Starting Position

    Foot Placement: Position feet slightly forward of the bar, about shoulder-width apart
    Bar Position: Rest the bar on your upper trapezius/shoulders, similar to a traditional back squat
    Stance: Feet can be slightly angled outward for natural hip rotation

Squat Movement

    Unrack the bar by rotating it to release from the safety hooks
    Slowly lower your body by bending knees and hips
    Descend until thighs are parallel to the ground (or slightly below)
    Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement
    Drive through your heels to return to the starting position

Benefits and Considerations
Advantages

    Reduced Balance Requirements: Fixed bar path makes it easier for beginners
    Controlled Movement: Less risk of improper form compared to free weight squats
    Isolation: Allows focused leg muscle development

translations.name: Abducción en máquina
translations.description:
Técnica y ejecución
Posición inicial
Posición sentada: Siéntese en la máquina con la espalda apoyada en la almohadilla. Colocación de las piernas: La parte interna de los muslos contra los puntos de resistencia acolchados de la máquina. Ajuste de la máquina: Ajuste la posición del asiento para alinear la articulación de la cadera con el punto de pivote de la máquina.

Técnica de movimiento
Comience con las piernas juntas o ligeramente presionadas contra las almohadillas internas. Empuje lentamente las piernas hacia afuera, separándolas. Muévase hasta alcanzar el máximo rango lateral cómodo. Haga una breve pausa en el punto final del movimiento. Regrese lentamente a la posición inicial con un movimiento controlado.

Desglose biomecánico
Activación muscular
Activación principal: Glúteo medio (músculo lateral de la cadera)
Estabilización: Activa los músculos del core y la zona lumbar.
Movimiento funcional: Imita el movimiento lateral de las piernas utilizado al caminar, practicar deportes y realizar actividades diarias.

Beneficios
Fuerza y ​​estabilidad
Mejora la estabilidad de la cadera
Reduce el riesgo de lesiones de rodilla
Mejora la capacidad de movimiento lateral
Trabaja grupos musculares a menudo descuidados

Rehabilitación y prevención
Beneficioso para:
Corredores
Atletas de deportes de movimiento lateral
Personas que se recuperan de lesiones en la parte inferior del cuerpo
Prevención de la desalineación de cadera y rodilla

translations.name: Machine Hip Abduction
translations.description:
Technique and Execution
Starting Position
    Seated Positioning: Sit in the machine with back against the pad
    Leg Placement: Inner thighs against the machine's padded resistance points
    Adjust Machine: Set seat position to align your hip joint with the machine's pivot point

Movement Technique
    Start with legs together or slightly pressed against the inner pads
    Slowly push legs outward, spreading them apart
    Move until maximum comfortable lateral range is reached
    Pause briefly at the outer point of the movement
    Slowly return to the starting position with controlled movement

Biomechanical Breakdown

Muscle Engagement
    Primary Activation: Gluteus Medius (side hip muscle)
    Stabilization: Engages core and lower back muscles
    Functional Movement: Mimics lateral leg movement used in walking, sports, and daily activities

Benefits
Strength and Stability
    Improves Hip Stability
    Reduces Risk of Knee Injuries
    Enhances Lateral Movement Capabilities
    Targets Often-Neglected Muscle Groups

translations.name: Fondos en maquina
translations.description:
El press de tríceps sentado es un ejercicio de entrenamiento de resistencia específico, diseñado específicamente para desarrollar y fortalecer los tríceps. 
Ajuste la altura del asiento para que las manijas de la máquina se alineen con la articulación del codo
Asegúrese de que la espalda esté firmemente apoyada contra el respaldo
Los pies deben estar apoyados en el suelo
Mantenga una posición neutra de la columna
Posición inicial
Sujete las manijas con un agarre neutro
Mantenga los brazos pegados al cuerpo
Codos doblados aproximadamente a 90 grados
Movimiento de prensa
Extienda los brazos hacia adelante empujando las manijas hacia afuera
Concéntrese en usar los tríceps para impulsar el movimiento
Evite usar los músculos del hombro o el pecho
Extienda completamente los brazos sin bloquear los codos
Regrese lentamente a la posición inicial
Mantenga un movimiento controlado y suave
Mantenga la tensión en los tríceps durante todo el ejercicio

translations.name: Seated Triceps Press
translations.description:
Machine Setup
    Adjust the seat height so that the machine's handles align with your elbow joint
    Ensure your back is firmly supported against the backrest
    Feet should be flat on the floor
    Maintain a neutral spine position

Movement Mechanics
    Starting Position
    Grip the handles with a neutral grip
    Keep upper arms close to your body
    Elbows bent at approximately 90 degrees

    Press Movement
    Extend arms forward by pushing the handles away
    Focus on using triceps to drive the movement
    Avoid using shoulder or chest muscles
    Fully extend arms without locking elbows

    Return Phase
    Slowly return to the starting position
    Maintain controlled, smooth motion
    Keep tension on the triceps throughout

translations.name: Romanian Deadlift
translations.description: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in each hand and bending forward at the hips while keeping the back straight, then returning to a standing position. It primarily targets the hamstrings and glutes.

translations.name: Cable pull through
translations.description:
The Cable Pull Through is a lower-body exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings using a "hip hinge" motion. To perform it, attach a rope to the lowest pulley setting, face away from the machine with the rope between your legs, and step forward to create tension. Keeping your back flat and knees slightly soft, push your hips backward as if trying to close a door behind you until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.

To finish the rep, explosively drive your hips forward to return to a standing position, squeezing your glutes hard at the top. It is vital to remember that this is not a squat; your knees should not bend deeply, and your arms should remain straight and relaxed, acting only as hooks for the rope. This movement provides constant tension on the glutes while placing less stress on the lower back than a traditional deadlift.

translations.name: Side lateral raise - Front (Cable)
translations.description: in a cablerack do a side lateral raise with cable in front of you, with focus in tension on the back of your shoulder.

translations.name: Side lateral raise - Back (Cable)
translations.description: on a cable rack do a side lateral raise with the cable behind you. focus on tension in the front of the shoulder.

translations.name: 45° lateral raises
translations.description: dumbbell raise in the space between front and side raises

translations.name: Shoulder Y-pull cable
translations.description: on a cable rack cross the cables and pull elbows to ceiling close to the body.

translations.name: supino
translations.description: Colocar a barra em linha reta ao peito e descer ate atingir 90 graus

translations.name: supino
translations.description: afafaahahhahahahahahahahahhahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahhahha

translations.name: supino
translations.description: Exercise description....................

translations.name: supino
translations.description: Exercise description....................

translations.name: Pull-Ups (Wide Grip)
translations.description:
The Pull-Up is a bodyweight exercise that builds strength in your back, shoulders, and biceps. It can be difficult at first, but you can make it easier by using bands or an assisted machine.

Instructions

  1. Grab the bar: Place your hands on the bar with your palms facing away from you.

  2. Get ready: Take a breath and tighten your stomach muscles.

  3. Pull up: Pull your body up until your chest reaches the bar. Think about driving your elbows down.

  4. Lower down: Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.

translations.name: Plank Reach
translations.description: Position your body in plank position, keeping your eyes on the ground. Raise one arm in front of you and return it. Repeat with the other arm. Raise and return slowly while keeping your body as still as possible.

translations.name: Unterarmstütz mit Armheben
translations.description: Bringe deinen Körper in die Planken-Position. Halte den Blick auf den Boden gerichtet und hebe abwechselnd deine Arme langsam nach vorne und zurück. Versuche dabei deinen Körper so ruhig wie möglich zu halten.

translations.name: bicep curl
translations.description: Não se encontra uma descrição disponível

translations.name: bicep
translations.description: Não se encontra uma descrição disponível

translations.name: cabel
translations.description: Não se encontra uma descrição disponível

translations.name: Seated machine row
translations.description: A seated row machine is a gym apparatus for strength training the back (lats, rhomboids, traps) by pulling handles toward the torso from a seated position, featuring adjustable seats and chest pads for proper form, emphasizing upright posture and squeezing shoulder blades, crucial for posture and back thickness, and distinct from a cardio rowing machine.

translations.name: Wrist curl, cable
translations.description:
1. Extend your arm down, then slightly lift your forearm
  2. Extend your wrist in a supinated form (palm up)
  3. Adjust cable height so that the cable handle is in your supinated hand
  4. Perform the movement by curling your palms and wrists upwards
  5. (optional/variable) Pause at the top
  6. Slowly return to the starting position

translations.name: Reverse crunch
translations.description:
1. Lay down
  2. Place your hands near your butt (often slightly in front or under or both)
  3. Raise your legs, but about 70% of the way you should be able to also raise your pelvis
  4. (optional/variable) Hold 1/2 seconds at the top
  5. Go back to starting position

notes.comment: Inclining (legs lower than head), or holding weight between/on your feet increases the difficulty

translations.name: Legend Incline Bench Press
translations.description: incline bench press on LeverEdge machine.

translations.name: Chair dips
translations.description:
Starting position:

Sit down on the front edge of a chair, back straight, hands holding the front edge. Still holding the edge of the chair, arms extended, lift your butt and walk forward slightly so that it is a few inches from the chair.

Steps:

1. Slowly lower your body, keeping the back straight, until your arms are at a right angle.
2. Raise your body again to the initial position, arms extended.
3. Repeat.

translations.name: Pec deck rear delt fly
translations.description: Seated rear delt fly on pec deck machine (facing the machine and extending arms backwards, the hands should be at the height of the shoulders)

translations.name: Barbell squat
translations.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting securely on your upper back (not your neck), gripping it slightly wider than shoulder-width. Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.

Begin the movement by bending at your hips and knees, lowering your body as if sitting back into a chair. Continue descending until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground (or slightly below if mobility allows). Keep your knees aligned with your toes and your weight evenly distributed through your heels.

Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees until you are standing tall again.

translations.name: Kniebeuge mit Kurzhanteln
translations.description:
• Muskulatur anspannen
  • Kein Schwung holen
  • Kontrollierte Bewegung
  • Langsame Bewegung
  • Beine nicht überstrecken
  • Rücken gerade halten

translations.name: Squat con bilanciere
translations.description:
Stai in piedi con i piedi alla larghezza delle spalle e il bilanciere appoggiato saldamente sulla parte alta della schiena (non sul collo), afferrandolo con le mani leggermente più larghe delle spalle. Mantieni il petto alto, la schiena dritta e il core contratto.

Inizia il movimento piegando fianchi e ginocchia, abbassando il corpo come se stessi per sederti su una sedia. Continua a scendere finché le cosce non sono almeno parallele al pavimento (o leggermente più in basso se la mobilità lo consente). Mantieni le ginocchia allineate con le punte dei piedi e il peso distribuito sui talloni.

Spingi con forza attraverso i talloni per tornare alla posizione iniziale, estendendo anche fianchi e ginocchia fino a tornare in posizione eretta.

translations.name: Suitcase Carry
translations.description: Walk forward and backward with holding dumbbell on one side without leaning to counter the weight.

translations.name: Deficit Push ups
translations.description: Pushup on blocks or grips, so you can dip lower than the hands in the decent.

translations.name: Butterfly Stretch
translations.description:
1. Sit in a prayer position with the soles of your feet gently pressed together.
  2. Use your hands to slowly pull your feet inwards towards your groin. Hold for as long as required.

notes.comment: Keep your back straight, don't enter a hollow position.
Push your thighs and knees downwards with your elbows if you need a better stretch.

translations.name: Frog Stretch
translations.description:
1. Get on your hands and knees.
  2. Widen your knees outwards, and drop to your elbows.
  3. Without moving your elbows or knees, start to rock forwards and backwards, trying to get your bum closer to your feet.

notes.comment: Maintain a gentle arch in your lower back, don't let it round/hollow.
For extra challenge, hold your shins parallel and point your feet outwards at a 90 degree angle from your body,

translations.name: Supino inclinado
translations.description: Inclinado 90 graus e movimentos leves e precisos

translations.name: Landmine Rotation
translations.description: Start with landmine barbell straight up by using your shoulders to push it up above you. Then, from that position, bring it your side to target your abdominals and return to starting position.

translations.name: unilateral cross body cable pull down
translations.description: on a cable rack place cable high, and with a straight arm pull that cable across you from high to low. focus on tension in back shoulder.

translations.name: Supino Inclinado
translations.description: Ângulo de 90 graus devagar..............

translations.name: Barbell Full Squat
translations.description:
• The barbell full squat is a compound exercise that targets multiple muscle groups in the lower body, including the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes.
  • Proper form is crucial for maximizing results and preventing injuries during the barbell full squat. This includes maintaining a shoulder-width stance, creating whole body tension, controlling your descent, and maintaining proper depth and knee positioning.
  • Assistance moves such as the front squat, goblet squat, split squat, and Bulgarian split squat can help improve your performance in the barbell full squat by targeting specific muscle groups and improving overall technique.
  • To achieve new personal records in your back squat, gradually increase weight over time, vary rep ranges and sets to stimulate muscle growth, and prioritize rest and recovery between training sessions.

notes.comment: Start by positioning your feet shoulder-width apart, with toes slightly turned outwards.

translations.name: Trap Bar Squat
translations.description: A combination of squat and deadlift principles with the use of a hex/trap bar, easing stress on the lower back by better centering the weight.

translations.name: Dorsiflexión de tobillo
translations.description: Mueve tu rodilla adelante evitando separar el talón del suelo. Manten la posición un par de segundos y vuelve a  la posición inicial

translations.name: ankle dorsiflexion rocks
translations.description: Move your knee forward, keeping your heel on the ground. Hold the position for a couple of seconds and return to the starting position.

translations.name: Barbell Squat
translations.description: A barbell squat is a fundamental strength exercise where you lower your body as if sitting in a chair while holding a weighted barbell, primarily targeting leg and core muscles, requiring proper form (chest up, back straight, knees over toes) for safety and effectiveness, engaging quads, hamstrings, glutes, and core for strength, muscle, and fat reduction.

notes.comment: To perform it, unrack the bar, step back, squat down by sitting back and keeping your chest high, then drive up through your feet, ensuring your knees track with your toes.

translations.name: Lat Pull Down
translations.description: The lat pull down is an exercise used to build the muscles of the back. While the exercise will primarily target the lats, you will also notice a fair amount of bicep and middle back activation.

translations.name: Behind the Back Cable Lateral Raise
translations.description: Lateral raises have long been a staple for building capped delts, and lifters use everything from dumbbells and bands to machines and single-arm variations to make them grow.

translations.name: Parallel Bar Hold
translations.description:
1. Stand between a set of parallel bars.
  2. Place your hands, knuckles outwards, on the bars.
  3. Push down on your hands to lift yourself off the floor.
  4. Hold yourself in this position with your arms straight for the required time.

notes.comment: Keep your body in a slightly hollow position

translations.name: Reverse Hyperextension
translations.description:
Lie face-down on a bench with your legs hanging off the edge.
  1. Bend your hips so your thighs are vertical, bending your knees at the same time so your shins stay horizontal.
  2. Straighten out your legs so they are not bent and your toes are pointing backwards horizontally.
  3. Return to the original position, and repeat.

translations.name: Sphinx
translations.description: Flach auf den Bauch legen, Beine gestreckt und Fußrücken am Boden. Bringe die Unterarme unter die Schultern, Ellenbogen direkt darunter. Dann drücke die Hände auf den Fußboden um den Brustkorb anzuheben. Ziehe die Schulterblätter zusammen, öffne die Schlüsselbeine und richte den Blick nach vorn, während der untere Rücken entspannt bleibt.

translations.name: Sphinx
translations.description:
Lie on your stomach flat, legs extended and the backs of your feet on 
the ground. Bring your forearms under your shoulders, elbows directly 
underneath. Then press your hands into the floor to lift your chest. 
Draw your shoulder blades together, open your collarbones, and direct 
your gaze forward, while keeping the lower back relaxed.

translations.name: Open Book
translations.description:
The Open Book is a kneeling torso twist that enhances thoracic mobility and spinal segmentation while opening the shoulders and chest. It emphasizes a stable lower body, controlled breath-guided rotation, and segmental spinal control rather than force or range of motion.

Position yourself kneeling on the floor with your right side facing a wall. Ensure your hips are square and stable, with the left knee down and the right foot forward. Lengthen your spine and align your head comfortably. Begin with your arms relaxed and open in front of your chest, palms facing each other and hands apart.

Inhale to initiate the movement, allowing your left arm —the one farther from the wall— to open outward and backward. Lead the twist from your thoracic spine, rotating your upper back progressively while keeping your pelvis still. Exhale as you complete the rotation, guiding your left hand toward the wall as your gaze follows your hand. Return by inhaling and slowly bringing your arm forward, reversing the spinal rotation segment by segment. Repeat on the opposite side.

translations.name: Clamshell
translations.description:
Position
Side plank with the elbow and the forearm on the ground and the legs slightly bent. 

Execution
Lift your upper knee while keeping your feet close together, like a “shell opening,” then slowly return to the starting position. Keep your core stable and your hips aligned.

translations.name: Clamshell
translations.description:
Posizione
Plank laterale, avambraccio a terra, corpo in linea retta. 

Esecuzione
Solleva il ginocchio superiore mantenendo i piedi vicini, come un “guscio che si apre”, poi ritorna lentamente. Mantieni il core stabile e i fianchi allineati.

translations.name: Dumbell Tate Press
translations.description:
1. Set Up: Lie flat with a dumbbell in each hand. Extend your arms straight up over your chest as if you were at the top of a dumbbell bench press
  2. Starting Position: Position the dumbbells so they are touching or very close together with a pronated grip (palms facing your feet and thumbs facing each other)
  3. Lowering: Without moving your upper arms or shoulders, bend your elbows and flare them outward to the sides. Lower the inner ends of the dumbbells toward the center of your chest
  4. Touch and Pause: Gently touch the dumbbells to your chest. Do not rest the weight on your body; maintain constant tension in your triceps
  5. Press: Forcefully extend your elbows to return the dumbbells to the starting position. Focus on "pushing your pinkies toward the sky" to maximize triceps engagement

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IgSjoXbpy1M&t=2

notes.comment: Upper Arm Stability: Your upper arms should remain mostly vertical. Avoid letting your shoulders round forward or move as you bend your elbows
Keep Elbows Out: Unlike a standard press or skull crusher, the elbows must point strictly outward to the sides throughout the movement
Wrist Alignment: Keep your wrists straight and firm to prevent strain

translations.name: Chest-Supported Rear Delt Raise
translations.description: "The Y"Position: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie face-down with your chest supported, keeping your head neutral or looking slightly down to avoid neck strain.Grip: Hold light dumbbells with a neutral or thumbs-up grip (thumb pointing to the ceiling).Movement: With straight or slightly bent elbows, raise the dumbbells up and out in a "Y" shape. Focus on moving the shoulders and shoulder blades rather than just lifting the arms.Top Position: Squeeze the shoulder blades together and reach high, ensuring the arms are angled to form a "Y".Control: Lower the dumbbells slowly to the starting position.

translations.name: Band pull-apart with external rotation
translations.description:
Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, chest out, and shoulders back.

Hold the resistance band with both hands, palms facing up or facing each other, depending on the band type.

Bend your elbows at a 90-degree angle and glue your upper arms to your sides.

The band should have some tension in the starting position, with your hands closer together in front of your stomach.

Rotate Outward: While keeping your elbows tucked into your sides, slowly move your hands away from each other, stretching the band.

Squeeze: Squeeze your shoulder blades together and down as you rotate your hands outward.

Range of Motion: Rotate your hands as far outward as you comfortably can without allowing your elbows to leave your sides.

Hold: Hold the end position for 1–2 seconds to maximize muscle engagement.

Return: Slowly and with control, bring your hands back to the starting position.

notes.comment: Consider placing a small, rolled-up towel between your elbow and your ribs on each side to ensure your elbows stay locked at your sides.

translations.name: Double-Leg Abdominal Press
translations.description:
Lie on your back with knees and hips bent at 90 degrees (tabletop).

Place your hands on your thighs just above your knees. Push your hands against your knees as hard as possible while using your abs to pull your knees toward your hands.

Hold for 10 seconds of maximum effort. Your abs should be shaking.

translations.name: Abdominal Draw-In
translations.description: Lie on your back with knees bent. Gently pull your belly button toward your spine without moving your hips or holding your breath.

translations.name: Landmine Squat to Press
translations.description:
Bar Position: Anchor one end of a barbell in a landmine attachment or a secure corner.
Stance: Stand facing the barbell with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Turn your toes out 10–35 degrees to allow for better pelvic movement and depth.
Grip: Cup the free end of the barbell with both hands (fingers interlaced or overlapping) and hold it at mid-chest level, just below the collarbone. 

Descent: Brace your core and sit back into a squat, keeping your chest up and weight on your heels.
Depth: Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor. A good reference is when your elbows touch the tops of your thighs or just inside your knees.
Posture: Ensure your back remains flat and your spine neutral throughout the movement. 

Drive: Powerfully drive through your heels to stand up. Use the momentum from your legs to "thrust" the weight upward.
Extension: In one fluid motion, press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended. At the top, your biceps should be near your ears.
The "Lean": As you reach the top of the press, lean slightly forward into the bar to encourage proper upward rotation of the shoulder blades and avoid lower back arching. 

Slowly lower the barbell back to chest height in a controlled manner before immediately beginning the next repetition.

translations.name: Barbell Step Back Lunge
translations.description:
Step 1: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell on your upper back.
    Step 2: Keep your back straight, chest up, and core engaged.
    Step 3: Take a step back with one leg and lower your body by bending both knees. Ensure that your back knee nearly touches the ground.
    Step 4: Push off your back foot to return to the starting position.
    Step 5: Repeat the movement with the other leg.
    Continue alternating between legs for the desired repetitions.

translations.name: Hammerstrength Decline Chest Press
translations.description:
1. Sit upright in a hammerstrength decline chest press machine while squeezing your shoulder blades together and placing your heels firmly on the ground underneath your knees.

2. The back pad should be contact with your head, shoulders, and butt at all times.

3. Grab ahold of the handles with an overhand grip just outside shoulder-width apart just below your chest.

4. Keeping your core braced by breathing into your stomach and flexing the abdominal muscles, push through your palms to extend your elbows while keeping them at a 45 degree angle from your torso.

5. Once your arms are fully extended, return to the starting position.

translations.name: Ring Support Hold
translations.description: Hold yourself in a support position on gymnastic rings with arms straight at sides. Focus on keeping rings turned out slightly and maintaining hollow body position. This is an isometric hold for chest, shoulders, and triceps.

translations.name: Floor Glider Hamstring Curls
translations.description: Lie on back with heels on floor gliders or towels. Lift hips into a bridge, then slide heels toward glutes while keeping hips elevated. Slowly return to the starting position while maintaining the hip bridge.

translations.name: Trap-3 Raise
translations.description:
1. Hinge forwards and bend your knees slightly. Depress and retract the shoulder you will use. Rest the other hand on your knee.
  2. Raise your hand about 15 degrees overhead, maintaining the position of your shoulder.

translations.name: External Rotation Stretch
translations.description:
1. Sit with your feet pressed against each other, and one knee raised all the way out to the side of your body.
  2. Place your elbow on your raised knee, bend it at 90 degrees, and point your hand directly forward, holding your barbell/dumbbell.
  3. Keep your shoulder depressed and retracted, raise your hand until your forearm is vertical, as if you were about to wave at someone like a robot.
  4. Lower your hand to the original position and repeat.

notes.comment: Shoulders Depressed and Retracted.
If you can’t feel it, don’t up the weight because it must be ‘too easy’.

translations.name: Banded Shoulder Drills
translations.description:
1. Attach a resistance band to something solid behind you. Stand away from it, holding it in your hand so it is taut.
  2. See the attached youtube video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zdwEWchSjrI .

translations.name: Solo Hip Flexor Stretch
translations.description: See this instructive video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHwyBHS6MQs .

translations.name: Bent-Leg Hamstring Stretch
translations.description: See this instructional video from Stretch Therapy: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CrF2iMnn09w .

translations.name: Seated Shoulder Extension Stretch
translations.description:
1. Sit with your hands behind you, shoulder width apart.
  2. Lock your elbows.
  3. Scoot your feet and hips out forwards, lowering your shoulders down towards ground.
  4. Go as far as possible to obtain a good stretch.
See the video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ihUAbG0e8zw

notes.comment: Be mindful of the elbows
Slow and controlled
Should not be painful

translations.name: Banded Clamshell
translations.description:
Position
Side plank with the elbow and the forearm on the ground and the legs slightly bent. Stretch a doubled-up resistance band between your thighs, just above the knee.

Execution
Lift your upper knee while keeping your feet close together, like a “shell opening,” then slowly return to the starting position. Keep your core stable and your hips aligned.

translations.name: Standing Pancake Good Morning
translations.description: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward. Place your hands on the back of your head. Hinge at your hips and lower your upper body towards the ground, keeping your back straight and chest open.

notes.comment: Keep your back straight and chest open throughout the movement

translations.name: Seated Pancake Good Morning
translations.description:
1. Sit with your legs completely straight (knees locked), spread wide in a straddle position.
  2. Place your hands on the back of your neck.
  3. Hinge your torso and head towards the floor.

notes.comment: Keep your back as flat as possible.
Initiate the ascent/return with your lower back, keeping your chest up.

translations.name: Horse Stance (Side Splits)
translations.description:
1. Stand as for a squat, with your feet wide and your toes pointed slightly outwards.
  2. Gently squat into a deep, wide squat - your hips should be below your knees.
  3. Hold for 3 to 5 seconds.
  4. Come up under control, and repeat as needed.

notes.comment: Keep your back straight, as with squats.
For a better stretch, widen your stance.

translations.name: Straddle L-Sit
translations.description:
With your legs in a sitting saddle position, push your body upwards off the ground. Your legs should be horizontal and point straight outwards. Your arms should be between your legs.

Hold isometrically as long as required.

notes.comment: Maintain a hollow body shape.
Protract and depress your shoulders, and lock your elbows.
Point your toes.

translations.name: L-sit
translations.description:
Sit on the ground with your legs together and your arms by your sides. Push your body off the ground using your hands, maintaining the same sitting position so that your legs are straight and your feet do not touch the floor.

Hold for as long as required.

notes.comment: Keep your shoulders depressed - do not allow yourself to shrug.
Keep your arms straight.

translations.name: L-Sit (Foot Supported)
translations.description: As with an L-sit, but allow your feet to touch the floor to support the some of the weight of your legs.

translations.name: Calves foam roller
translations.description: Move your calves slowly over the foam roller

translations.name: Foam Roller Gemelos
translations.description: Mueve sobre el foam roller tus gemelos lentamente

translations.name: Foam Roller isquitobiales
translations.description: Mueve sobre el foam roller tus isquitobiales lentamente

translations.name: Hamstring Foam roller
translations.description: Move your hamstrings slowly over the foam roller

translations.name: Foam Roller Anterior tibialis
translations.description: Use the foam roller slowly in the tibial anterioris. This is english, please

translations.name: Foam Roller Tibial anterior
translations.description: Deslice el tibial anterior lentamente sobre el Foam Roller

translations.name: Foam Roller Cintillar iliotibial
translations.description: Deslizate sobr el Foam Roller sobre tu Cintilla iliotibial

translations.name: Foam Roller Iliotibial band
translations.description: Slide on the Foam Roller over your iliotibial band. Is english terrible checker

translations.name: Foam Roller cuadriceps
translations.description: Deslizate sobre el Foam Roller sobre tus cuadriceps

translations.name: Foam Roller quadriceps
translations.description: Slide the Foam Roller over your quadriceps. Is english, please

translations.name: Foam Roller Gluteus
translations.description: Slide on the Foam Roller over your gluteus. Is english please

translations.description: Deslizate sobre el Foam Roller sobre tu glúteo. Es castellano por favor corrector

translations.name: Foam Roller Adductors
translations.description: Slide the Foam Roller over your Adductors. Is english please

translations.name: Foam Roller Aductores
translations.description: Deslizate sobre el Foam Roller sobre tus Aductores. Es castellano por favor
